docs.title: UI & HUD Subsystems — HUD Modules, Galaxy Map & Menus
Overview
docs.text: The HUD system is a modular, lazy-loaded CanvasLayer that provides flight information, targeting feedback, economy data, and game state awareness. The architecture was designed to solve the problem of a monolithic 1881-line HudOverlay script blocking startup — widgets are now loaded across frames using a priority-queued bootstrap system so combat-critical elements appear immediately while expensive widgets load progressively.

docs.title: HudOverlay — Main HUD CanvasLayer
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/ui/hud_overlay.gd (1256+ lines), HudOverlay is the root CanvasLayer for all in-game UI. It extends Godot's CanvasLayer and registers itself in groups "ui/hud_overlay" and "hud/runtime_refreshable".

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: The HUD follows a coordinator pattern: HudOverlay manages layout containers, service connections, and widget lifecycle, but individual widgets are instantiated via factory methods and loaded through the priority bootstrap system.

docs.title: Scene Structure (Layout Containers)
docs.text: Container Type Purpose %HudPanel PanelContainer Main HUD panel wrapper %WidgetRow HBoxContainer Navigation widgets row (navball, radar, assist vector) %WeaponWidgetBox VBoxContainer Weapon/countermeasure display area %ThrottleBoostVbox VBoxContainer Throttle and boost indicators %ShipInfoContainer VBoxContainer Ship info panel content %ShipInformation PanelContainer Ship info panel wrapper (toggleable) %CargoPanel / %CargoInfo PanelContainer/VBoxContainer Cargo display area %BoostInfo VBoxContainer Boost/travel mode indicators %WeaponInfo VBoxContainer Weapon status (overheat, ammo, plasma charge) %ShieldInfo / %HullInfo / %FuelInfo VBoxContainer Defense/status bars %MiningInfo VBoxContainer Mining/limpet display area %CreditsLabel / %ResourcesLabel Label Economy strip (always visible) %EconomyDetailsLabel Label Expanded economy details (toggleable) %IntelDock Control Utility buttons (legend, journal, cargo manifest, jobs) %DiscoveryLegend / %LegendLabel PanelContainer/Label Discovery info display %FpsAnchor / %FpsLabel Control/Label FPS counter

docs.title: Widget Visibility Toggles
docs.text:
All visibility is controlled via @export bools on HudOverlay, defaulting to true except debug panel: 

@export var show_navball: bool = true
@export var load_navball_during_join: bool = false  # Deferred loading for navball
@export var show_radar_x4: bool = true
@export var show_assist_vector: bool = true
@export var show_target_indicator: bool = true
@export var show_targeting_reticle: bool = true
@export var show_jump_indicator: bool = true
@export var show_weapon_groups: bool = true
@export var show_countermeasures: bool = true
@export var show_debug_panel: bool = false
@export var show_hud_panel: bool = true

docs.title: HudContext Enum
docs.text:
The HUD supports three contexts that control which widgets are visible: 

enum HudContext {
    CONTEXT_FLIGHT,   # Navball, radar, weapons, throttle, jump indicator, targeting reticle
    CONTEXT_DOCKED,   # Cargo manifest, economy strip, intel dock, jobs board
    CONTEXT_MENU,     # Minimal shell only
}
var _current_context: HudContext = HudContext.CONTEXT_FLIGHT

docs.title: Service Modules (Child Nodes)
docs.text: Three service modules are instantiated as child nodes during _ready(): 1. HudEconomyModule — Credits display, resource ledger, economy strip toggle 2. HudFactionModule — Faction standing notifications and UI updates 3. HudJobsModule — Discovery journal, cargo manifest modal, jobs board, discovery notifications

docs.title: Key Methods
docs.text:
func _ready() -> void                          # Setup containers, connect services, start bootstrap on next frame
func _bootstrap_dynamic_widgets() -> void      # Create HudWidgetBootstrap and register all widgets
func _register_widget_bootstraps() -> void     # Register 24+ widgets with priorities (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
func _unhandled_input(event: InputEvent) -> void  # Handle HUD input actions (hail, journal, cargo manifest, jobs)

docs.title: Service Connections
docs.text: During _ready(), HudOverlay connects to: - TrackableRegistry — For trackable entity updates - TargetingManager — For target change events (target_changed signal) - CreditsManager (via EconomyModule) — For credit changes - FactionManager (via FactionModule) — For faction standing updates - SystemEventRuntime (via JobsModule) — For system event notifications

docs.title: HudWidgetBootstrap — Priority Queue System
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/ui/hud_widget_bootstrap.gd (147 lines), this is the core lazy-loading engine that replaces the old sequential widget instantiation chain. It loads widgets across multiple frames within a per-frame time budget, preventing HUD startup from blocking player control.

docs.title: Priority Levels
docs.text: Level Value Widgets Included Load Time Target CRITICAL 0 Targeting reticle, weapon groups, countermeasures, jump indicator, throttle boost, ship info, cargo, boost, travel mode, laser overheat, weapon ammo, plasma charge, limpet, mining, shield, hull, fuel First 2-3 frames (~5ms) HIGH 1 Navball, radar X4, assist vector, target indicator, target info, position Next 5-10 frames MEDIUM 2 Target view (SubViewport+Camera), navigation widget, debug panel Background / on-demand LOW 3 Discovery notification, active jobs, convoy waypoint, multiplayer debug As needed

docs.title: API
docs.text:
func register(id: String, priority: int, factory: Callable, visible_property: String = "") -> void
# Register a widget for lazy bootstrap. id is unique; factory returns the instantiated Node.

func start() -> void
# Sort pending queue by priority and begin frame-by-frame loading.

func process_next() -> bool
# Process next batch within time budget (default 5ms/frame). Returns true when all loaded.

func remove_widget(id: String) -> void
# Free a widget instance and remove from queue if not yet loaded.

func toggle_widget(id: String, enabled: bool) -> void
# Runtime enable/disable: pushes to front of pending queue on enable, frees instance on disable.

func is_loaded(id: String) -> bool
func get_instance(id: String) -> Node
func has_pending() -> bool
func is_running() -> bool

docs.title: Bootstrap Flow
docs.text:
1. _bootstrap_dynamic_widgets() creates HudWidgetBootstrap and calls _register_widget_bootstraps()
  2. Each widget is registered with a factory callable (from HudWidgetFactories) and an optional visibility property name
  3. start() sorts entries by priority into the pending queue
  4. On each frame, process_next() loads widgets until the time budget is exhausted
  5. Loaded widgets are added to _widgets array and synced with ship/trackables

docs.title: HudWidgetFactories — Static Widget Factories
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/ui/hud_widget_factories.gd (290 lines), provides static factory methods for instantiating each HUD widget. Each factory checks scene references, visibility flags, and container availability before creating the widget.

docs.title: Factory Methods
docs.text: Method Widget Container Priority factory_navball() Navball (3D viewport) nav_widget_row HIGH factory_radar_x4() Radar X4 nav_widget_row HIGH factory_assist_vector() Assist vector arrow nav_widget_row HIGH factory_target_indicator() Target direction indicator hud root (add_child) HIGH factory_targeting_reticle() Crosshair reticle hud root CRITICAL factory_jump_indicator() Jump sequence progress hud root CRITICAL factory_throttle_boost() Throttle/boost bars throttle_boost_vbox CRITICAL factory_ship_info() Ship name/class info ship_info_container CRITICAL factory_cargo() Cargo capacity display cargo_info CRITICAL factory_boost() Boost energy bar boost_info CRITICAL factory_travel_mode() Travel mode indicator boost_info CRITICAL factory_laser_overheat() Laser heat gauge weapon_info CRITICAL factory_weapon_ammo() Weapon ammo count weapon_info CRITICAL factory_plasma_charge() Plasma charge level weapon_info CRITICAL factory_weapon_groups() Active weapon group display weapon_widget_box CRITICAL factory_countermeasures() Countermeasure slots weapon_widget_box CRITICAL factory_shield() Shield status bar shield_info CRITICAL factory_hull() Hull integrity bar hull_info CRITICAL factory_fuel() Fuel level display fuel_info CRITICAL factory_limpet() Limpet count/status mining_info CRITICAL factory_mining() Mining tool status mining_info CRITICAL factory_target_view() Target view SubViewport+Camera3D hud root MEDIUM factory_navigation_widget() Galaxy route planner hud root MEDIUM factory_debug_panel() Debug overlay panel hud root MEDIUM factory_discovery_notification() Discovery alert popup hud root LOW factory_active_jobs() Active jobs list hud root LOW factory_convoy_waypoint() Convoy waypoint marker hud root LOW factory_mp_debug() Multiplayer debug overlay hud root LOW

docs.title: Scene References
docs.text:
Each factory uses a scene reference from HudOverlay's @export properties, with fallback to preloaded defaults: 

const DEFAULT_NAVBALL_SCENE := preload("res://scenes/ui/navball_control.tscn")
# ... 25+ more default scenes loaded at compile time

docs.title: HudEconomyModule
docs.text:
Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/ui/hud_economy_module.gd (237 lines), manages credits display and resource ledger.

Credits Display: Connects to CreditsManager.credits_changed signal, formats with comma separators via _format_credits().

Resource Ledger: Reads from ResourceLedgerManager.get_or_create_ledger() + GalaxyNavigator.get_system_coords() to show current system resources. Displays top 2 resources on the main strip, expandable to full list (up to 5 shown) when "Details" button is pressed.

Refresh Rate: Resource ledger refreshes every 1 second via _process().

docs.title: HudFactionModule
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/ui/hud_faction_module.gd, manages faction standing notifications and UI updates based on FactionManager signals.

docs.title: HudJobsModule
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/ui/hud_jobs_module.gd, manages the discovery journal, cargo manifest modal, jobs board, and discovery notification popups. Provides methods: - toggle_discovery_journal() — Open/close discovery log - toggle_cargo_manifest(ship) — Show cargo details for a ship - toggle_jobs_journal() — Open/close active jobs list - set_discovery_notification(control) — Reference to discovery popup node - set_active_jobs_widget(control) — Reference to active jobs panel

docs.title: Individual Widget Scenes
docs.text:
All widgets are defined as .tscn scene files in /home/j/3d-space/scenes/ui/:

Scene File Widget Class Purpose navball_control.tscn NavballWidget 3D attitude indicator (SubViewport + Camera3D) — ~40ms to instantiate radar_x4.tscn RadarX4Widget 4-quadrant radar display assist_vector_widget.tscn AssistVectorWidget Directional arrow for assist mode target_indicator.tscn TargetIndicatorWidget Arrow pointing toward target targeting_reticle_overlay.tscn TargetingReticleWidget Crosshair reticle overlay jump_indicator.tscn JumpIndicatorWidget Jump sequence progress bar weapon_group_widget.tscn WeaponGroupsWidget Active weapon group display countermeasure_widget.tscn CountermeasuresWidget Countermeasure slot indicators throttle_boost_widget.tscn ThrottleBoostWidget Throttle and boost energy bars boost_widget.tscn BoostWidget Boost energy level bar travel_mode_widget.tscn TravelModeWidget Travel mode status indicator laser_overheat_widget.tscn LaserOverheatWidget Laser heat gauge weapon_ammo_widget.tscn WeaponAmmoWidget Ammo count display plasma_charge_widget.tscn PlasmaChargeWidget Plasma charge level shield_widget.tscn ShieldWidget Shield status bar hull_widget.tscn HullWidget Hull integrity bar fuel_widget.tscn FuelWidget Fuel level display target_info_widget.tscn TargetInfoWidget Target name, distance, faction info position_widget.tscn PositionWidget Current position coordinates cargo_widget.tscn CargoWidget Cargo bay capacity and contents limpet_widget.tscn LimpetWidget Limpet count/status mining_widget.tscn MiningWidget Mining tool status target_view_widget.tscn TargetViewWidget SubViewport + Camera3D for target close-up view navigation_widget.tscn NavigationWidget Galaxy route planner (GalaxyRoutePlanner) debug_panel.tscn DebugPanel Debug overlay with performance stats discovery_notification.tscn DiscoveryNotification Popup notification for discoveries active_jobs_widget.tscn ActiveJobsWidget List of active jobs/missions cockpit_frame.tscn CockpitFrame Cockpit frame overlay (CanvasLayer)

docs.title: Input Actions Handled by HUD

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by flight-controller for flight status display (throttle, boost, travel mode)
  • Displays data from combat-system targeting (reticle, target info, weapon groups)
  • Shows ship-systems runtime state (hull, shield, fuel bars)
  • Integrated with networking-multiplayer debug overlay (MP debug widget)
  • Uses FW_ConfigService for UI visibility preferences
  • Driven by TargetingManager signals for target change updates

docs.title: VFX & Visual Effects System — Thruster Sockets, ShipVFX Manager & Flight Sync
Overview
docs.text: The VFX system provides real-time thruster trails, booster effects, and glow lighting for all ships in 3dSpace. It follows a manager → socket architecture where ShipVFX acts as the central coordinator that computes emission values from throttle/boost state and broadcasts them to individual ThrusterSocket instances. Each socket independently controls its own particle systems (GPUParticles3D) and glow nodes, enabling per-engine customization while maintaining unified visual behavior.

docs.title: ShipVFX — Central VFX Manager
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/vfx/ship_vfx.gd (600 lines), ShipVFX is a @tool Node3D that serves as the master controller for all ship visual effects. It reads throttle, boost, and travel mode state from the flight controller and computes per-socket emission values using configurable presets.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
FlightController → set_throttle() / set_boost() / set_travel_mode()
    ↓
ShipVFX._apply_state() — computes emission, glow_color, glow_energy, particle_scale
    ↓
For each ThrusterSocket: call("apply_state", mapped_throttle, boost, emission, glow_color, glow_energy, particle_scale)

docs.title: Key Properties
docs.text: Property Type Default Description preset ShipVFXPreset null Visual preset defining colors, multipliers, LOD distances sockets Array[NodePath] [] Explicit socket paths (fallback: auto-discovery) auto_duplicate_materials bool true Duplicate materials per-instance to prevent shared-state bugs use_environment_modifiers bool true Reduce emission in foggy environments always_visible_for_subviewports bool true Keep VFX visible in editor subviewport previews subviewport_lod_bias float 1.0 Multiplier for LOD distance calculations in subviewports preview_emission_boost float 1.4 Emission multiplier when _preview_mode is active (editor only) always_full_for_local_player bool true Always render full VFX for the local player's ship use_distance_culling bool true Reduce/disable VFX based on camera distance

docs.title: State Variables
docs.text:
var _throttle: float = 0.0          # Clamped 0-1, drives emission intensity
var _boost: bool = false            # Boost active → emission × boost_mult
var _travel_mode: bool = false      # Travel mode → emission × boost_mult × 1.5 + blue tint
var _preview_mode: bool = false     # Editor-only preview (safety check in _ready)
var _environment_density: float = 0.0  # Fog density from environment modifier
var _socket_nodes: Array[Node] = []   # Resolved ThrusterSocket instances

docs.title: Socket Discovery
docs.text:
_gather_sockets() uses a three-tier discovery strategy: 1. Explicit paths — Nodes listed in @export var sockets: Array[NodePath] 2. Self children — find_children("", "ThrusterSocket", true, false) and "LineThrusterSocket" on self 3. Parent children — Same search on parent node (handles cases where ShipVFX is a child of the ship root)

Each discovered socket must have an apply_state() method to be accepted into _socket_nodes. Invalid/null instances are pruned by _prune_invalid_sockets().

docs.title: Emission Computation Pipeline (_apply_state)
docs.text:
The emission calculation follows this exact sequence:

1. mapped_throttle = preset.throttle_curve.sample(_throttle)  # Custom curve or identity
2. emission_base = preset.emission_base (default 1.0 if no preset)
3. emission = mapped_throttle × emission_base
4. If _boost: emission *= preset.emission_boost_multiplier (default 5.0)
5. If _travel_mode: emission *= boost_mult × 1.5  # Even brighter than boost
6. lod_multiplier = _get_lod_multiplier() → emission *= lod_multiplier
7. glow_color = preset.glow_color (white default)
   - If _boost: use preset.boost_glow_color (orange default)
   - If _travel_mode: Color(0.2, 0.5, 1.0, 1.0) — blue tint
8. glow_energy = preset.glow_energy × emission
   - If _boost: *= preset.boost_glow_energy_multiplier (default 2.5)
   - If _travel_mode: *= 2.0
9. particle_scale = preset.particle_scale (default 1.0)
   - If _boost: *= preset.boost_particle_scale_multiplier (default 1.4)
   - If _travel_mode: *= 2.0
10. If _preview_mode: emission ×= preview_emission_boost, glow_energy ×= preview_emission_boost
11. If environment_density > 0: emission ×= clamp(1.0 - density, 0.2, 1.0)
12. Enforce min_emission = max(preset.min_emission, node-level min_emission)
13. Debug fallback: if preset == null and use_debug_fallback → emission = debug_fallback_emission (0.35), glow_energy = debug_fallback_glow_energy (14.0)

docs.title: LOD System (_get_lod_multiplier)
docs.text:
Distance-based VFX reduction to optimize rendering for distant ships:

Distance Range Multiplier Effect 0 – lod_mid_distance (200m default) 1.0 Full VFX lod_mid – lod_far_distance (600m default) 0.5 Half emission/particles lod_far – lod_cull_distance (800m default) 0.15 Minimal VFX > lod_cull_distance 0.0 Culled entirely

LOD distances are configurable per-preset (lod_mid_distance, lod_far_distance, lod_cull_distance). Manual override via force_lod(level) accepts: "near" (1.0), "mid" (0.5), "far" (0.15), "cull" (0.0).

docs.title: Environment Modifiers
docs.text: set_environment_modifier(density, color, exposure_bias) allows the environment system to reduce VFX intensity in foggy/dense atmospheres: - density (0–1): Fog density; emission × clamp(1 - density, 0.2, 1.0) - Applied only when use_environment_modifiers is true

docs.title: Debug System
docs.text: ShipVFX includes extensive debug capabilities: - trace_debug flag enables verbose logging at every socket application step - set_debug_override(force_visible, show_marker) forces all sockets to maximum visibility and spawns temporary emissive markers (3D sphere + 2D screen overlay) at each socket position - _spawn_temporary_marker(pos, duration=3.0) creates a bright orange MeshInstance3D + CanvasLayer ColorRect at the given world position for visual debugging

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
func apply_preset(new_preset: ShipVFXPreset) -> void          # Apply a new visual preset (null-safe)
func set_throttle(value: float) -> void                       # Set throttle 0-1, triggers _apply_state()
func set_boost(active: bool) -> void                          # Toggle boost mode
func set_travel_mode(active: bool) -> void                    # Toggle travel drive mode
func set_preview_mode(enabled: bool) -> void                  # Editor preview toggle
func register_preview_camera(cam: Camera3D) -> void           # Set camera for LOD calculations
func set_environment_modifier(density, color, exposure_bias=0.0) -> void  # Fog/atmosphere modifier
func force_lod(level: String) -> void                         # Override LOD level ("near"/"mid"/"far"/"cull")
func clear_forced_lod() -> void                               # Restore automatic LOD
func ensure_unique_materials() -> void                        # Duplicate materials on all sockets
func add_socket(socket: Node) -> void                         # Add a socket at runtime (auto-duplicates materials)
func set_debug_override(force_visible, show_marker) -> void   # Debug visibility override
func get_socket_count() -> int                                # Number of active sockets
func get_last_emission() -> float                             # Last computed emission value
func get_last_lod_multiplier() -> float                       # Last LOD multiplier applied

docs.title: ThrusterSocket — Per-Engine Particle Controller
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/vfx/thruster_socket.gd (860 lines), ThrusterSocket is a @tool Node3D that manages individual thruster particle systems and glow nodes. Each socket represents one engine nozzle on a ship and contains: - Thruster particles — Continuous GPUParticles3D for normal thrust trails - Booster particles — GPUParticles3D for boost-mode effects (more aggressive) - Glow node — Either an OmniLight3D or MeshInstance3D with emissive material

docs.title: Node Resolution (_resolve_nodes)
docs.text:
On _ready(), each socket resolves its child nodes by path or name: 

_thruster_particles = get_node_or_null(thruster_particles_path) as GPUParticles3D
    // fallback: find_child("ThrusterParticles")
_booster_particles = get_node_or_null(booster_particles_path) as GPUParticles3D
    // fallback: find_child("BoosterParticles")
_glow_node = get_node_or_null(glow_node_path)
    // fallback: find_child("ThrusterGlow")

docs.title: Auto-Registration with ShipVFX
docs.text: At runtime (not in editor), each ThrusterSocket automatically walks up the parent chain to find its nearest ShipVFX ancestor and registers itself via node.add_socket(self). This eliminates manual socket path configuration for most ship setups.

docs.title: Particle Configuration
docs.text:
Each particle system stores base parameters on resolution: 

_base_thruster_amount = _thruster_particles.amount
_base_thruster_speed = _thruster_particles.speed_scale
_base_thruster_preprocess = _thruster_particles.preprocess
_base_thruster_scale = _thruster_particles.scale

World-space enforcement: If particles have local_coords = true, the socket automatically switches them to world-space (local_coords = false), resets preprocess/explosiveness, and restarts. This ensures exhaust trails remain visible behind moving ships rather than being clipped by local coordinate bounds.

docs.title: Material Caching (_init_cached_resources)
docs.text:
For performance optimization, each socket caches: - Whether process materials support color_ramp (GradientTexture2D) - Cached Gradient + GradientTexture2D instances for fast color updates - Draw pass 1 material references for emission tinting

This avoids repeated property list scans on every frame update.

docs.title: apply_state() — Per-Socket Visual Update
docs.text:
Called by ShipVFX every frame with computed values:

func apply_state(
    mapped_throttle: float,      # Curve-mapped throttle (0-1)
    boost: bool,                 # Boost active flag
    emission: float,             # Final emission intensity
    glow_color: Color,           # Tint color for particles and glow
    glow_energy: float,          # Emission energy multiplier
    particle_scale: float        # Particle size scale factor
) -> void

Thrust particles: - amount = max(100, int(base_amount × emission)) — More throttle = more particles - speed_scale = max(0.1, base_speed × (0.35 + emission)) — Faster exhaust at higher throttle - scale = base_scale × particle_scale — Size scaling from VFX manager - On first emit: sets preprocess=0.0, explosiveness=0.0, snaps global_transform to socket position, restarts

Booster particles: (only when _boost == true) - amount = max(100, int(base_amount × emission × 2.8)) — 2.8× more particles during boost - speed_scale = max(0.1, base_speed × (1.0 + emission × 1.8)) — Faster exhaust - initial_velocity_min/max on process material for explosive look - scale = Vector2(particle_scale, particle_scale) on process material

Glow node: - If OmniLight3D: sets light_color = glow_color, light_energy = energy × local_glow_multiplier - If MeshInstance3D with StandardMaterial3D: sets emission = glow_color, emission_energy_multiplier = max(0, energy)

Color ramp optimization: When process materials support color_ramp, the socket uses cached GradientTexture2D instances for fast per-frame color updates instead of creating new gradients.

docs.title: Debug Features
docs.text:
• debug_force_visible: Forces particles to maximum amount (300), scale (4×), and glow multiplier (8×)
  • debug_show_marker: Spawns an emissive sphere at the socket position for visual debugging
  • _spawn_runtime_test_particles(): Creates a temporary GPUParticles3D at the socket position to verify rendering pipeline

docs.title: Booster-Only Sockets
docs.text: The is_booster_only flag allows sockets that only emit during boost mode (e.g., afterburner nozzles). When false and not boosting, emission is set to 0.

docs.title: ShipVFXPreset — Visual Configuration Resource
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/resources/ship_vfx_preset.gd (20 lines), this Resource class defines all visual parameters for a ship's VFX system. Multiple presets can be created and assigned to different ships or factions.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Default Description thruster_scene PackedScene null Custom thruster particle scene (optional override) booster_scene PackedScene null Custom booster particle scene (optional override) throttle_curve Curve new Curve() Custom throttle-to-emission mapping curve emission_base float 20.0 Base emission intensity multiplier emission_boost_multiplier float 5.0 Emission multiplier during boost glow_color Color (1.0, 0.9, 0.7) Normal thrust glow color (warm white) boost_glow_color Color (1.0, 0.4, 0.1) Boost-mode glow color (orange) glow_energy float 50.0 Base glow energy for light/mesh emission boost_glow_energy_multiplier float 2.5 Additional energy multiplier during boost particle_scale float 1.0 Base particle size scale boost_particle_scale_multiplier float 1.4 Particle scale multiplier during boost min_emission float 0.15 Minimum emission to keep VFX visible at idle boost_visual_threshold float 0.7 Throttle threshold for visual boost transition lod_near_distance float 50.0 Near LOD distance (unused, kept for compatibility) lod_mid_distance float 200.0 Mid LOD distance — full → half emission lod_far_distance float 600.0 Far LOD distance — half → minimal emission lod_cull_distance float 800.0 Cull distance — beyond this, VFX disabled

docs.title: Throttle Curve
docs.text: The throttle_curve: Curve property allows designers to create non-linear throttle responses. For example, a curve that ramps quickly at low throttle and flattens near full throttle creates more responsive-feeling thrust without changing actual flight physics. If no curve is set or it has zero points, the mapping is identity (output = input).

docs.title: FlightVfxSync — Integration Helper
docs.text:
Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/flight/flight_vfx_sync.gd (33 lines), this RefCounted class provides a static helper for synchronizing VFX state from flight controllers. It's used by both the player FlightController and NPC AI flight controllers to update ship VFX each frame.

static func sync_ship_vfx(
    ship_vfx: ShipVFX,
    throttle_value: float,
    boost_active: bool,
    include_debug_log: bool,
    vfx_debug: bool,
    vfx_debug_last_msec: int,
    controller_name: String,
    raw_throttle: float
) -> Dictionary

Returns a dictionary with debug logging status and VFX metrics (socket count, last emission, LOD multiplier). Debug logs are rate-limited to once per second.

docs.title: FlightController → ShipVFX
docs.text:
The player FlightController calls FlightVfxSync.sync_ship_vfx() every physics frame: 

var result := FlightVfxSync.sync_ship_vfx(
    ship_vfx, throttle_value, boost_active,
    include_debug_log, vfx_debug, _vfx_debug_last_msec,
    "PlayerController", raw_throttle
)

docs.title: ShipSystemsCoordinator → VFX Configuration
docs.text: ShipSystemsCoordinator.configure_from_ship_data() calls parent.call("configure_ship_vfx", sdata) on the ship node, which applies the preset from ShipData.vfx_preset.

docs.title: RemoteShipController (Multiplayer)
docs.text: Remote ships receive VFX state through snapshot data. The RemoteShipController applies throttle and boost values to remote ship instances' ShipVFX nodes, ensuring visual consistency across all clients.

docs.title: Key Files Reference
docs.text: File Purpose Lines scripts/vfx/ship_vfx.gd Central VFX manager — emission computation, LOD system, socket discovery, environment modifiers 600 scripts/vfx/thruster_socket.gd Per-engine particle controller — GPUParticles3D management, glow nodes, material caching, auto-registration 860 scripts/resources/ship_vfx_preset.gd Visual configuration resource — colors, multipliers, LOD distances, throttle curve 20 scripts/flight/flight_vfx_sync.gd Static sync helper for flight controllers → ShipVFX integration 33

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by flight-controller for real-time thruster visual feedback (throttle → emission mapping)
  • Configured via ship-systems ShipData.vfx_preset field and vfx_socket_paths node paths
  • Used by networking-multiplayer RemoteShipController to sync VFX state on remote ships
  • Integrated with ui-hud-subsystems debug panel (VFX emission values displayed in debug overlay)

docs.title: Weapons & Countermeasures — Types, Mechanics & Limpet Tasks
Overview
docs.text:
Combat in the game revolves around four distinct weapon types, each with unique tactical roles and mechanical behaviors. Weapons are organized into groups of up to 8 configurable slots, allowing players to switch between different loadouts mid-flight. Every weapon generates heat during use, creating a risk-reward cycle where sustained fire must be balanced against overheating penalties.

Defensive countermeasures operate independently from offensive weapons, providing automated or manual protection against incoming threats. Point defense systems intercept projectiles, while other countermeasure types (ECM, chaff) disrupt enemy targeting and sensor systems.

docs.title: Laser Weapons — Beam-Based Instant Hit
docs.text:
Laser weapons fire continuous beams of concentrated energy that strike their target instantly upon firing. There is no travel time, making lasers the most accurate weapon type at any range — accuracy depends entirely on pilot skill in keeping the beam centered on the target.

Tactical Role: Consistent damage output, effective against fast-moving targets, requires sustained aim for maximum effect.

Property Default Description damage 5.0 Damage per tick while the beam is active (typically 0.2s intervals) ramp_time 1.5 Seconds to reach full power from cold start — early shots do reduced damage weapon_range 1,200 Maximum effective range; beams don't extend beyond this distance

Visual Properties: | Property | Default | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | beam_color | Red #FF3333 | Color of the laser beam visual | | beam_width | 0.05 | Thickness of the beam cylinder | | beam_style | "solid" | Rendering style (solid, pulsing, split) |

Beam Effects: | Property | Default | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | pulse_rate | 0.0 | Frequency of visual pulse waves traveling along the beam | | pulse_strength | 0.6 | Intensity of the pulse wave effect | | pulse_count | 8 | Number of simultaneous pulse waves on the beam | | pulse_speed | 6.0 | Speed at which pulses travel from muzzle to target | | beam_split_count | 1 | Number of parallel beams (for multi-barrel weapons) | | beam_split_separation | 0.05 | Lateral spacing between split beams |

Heat Management: | Property | Default | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | heat_capacity | 100.0 | Total heat units before overheating occurs | | heat_rate | 25.0 | Heat generated per second while firing | | heat_recharge | 20.0 | Heat dissipation rate when not firing (units/second) | | heat_recharge_delay | 1.0 | Seconds of cooling pause before heat starts dissipating |

Overheat Mechanics: When a weapon's accumulated heat reaches its capacity, it enters an overheat state: - Firing is temporarily disabled until heat drops below the resume threshold (overheat_resume_threshold_pct, default 25%) - Heat dissipation slows significantly during overheat (overheat_recharge_multiplier, default 0.25× normal rate) - An optional enforced cooldown period may apply (overheat_cooldown, default 2 seconds)

Charged Shots: Some lasers support holding fire to charge for a more powerful shot: | Property | Default | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | charge_time_max | 3.0 | Maximum charge time in seconds | | charged_damage_multiplier | 3.0 | Damage multiplier for fully charged shots | | charged_cooldown_multiplier | 2.5 | Heat penalty multiplier after firing a charged shot |

Gimbaled Mounting: Weapons can be mounted on gimbals that track targets independently of ship orientation: | Property | Default | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | gimbaled | false | Whether the weapon tracks targets automatically | | gimbal_turn_rate | 6.0 | Maximum rotation speed for gimbaled tracking (degrees/second) |

Gimbaled weapons are essential for fighting enemies that maneuver unpredictably, as they maintain aim without requiring the pilot to point their ship at the target.

docs.title: Plasma Weapons — Projectile-Based with Travel Time
docs.text:
Plasma weapons fire glowing projectiles of superheated ionized gas that travel through space before impacting their target. The projectile's flight time creates a skill gap: skilled pilots can dodge plasma bolts by maneuvering, while unmaneuverable targets take guaranteed damage.

Tactical Role: High single-hit damage, effective against slow or large targets, requires leading moving targets.

Property Default Description speed 220.0 Projectile travel speed (game units/second) — faster = easier to hit damage 12.0 Damage dealt on impact with target or shield max_lifetime 6.0 Maximum flight time before the projectile self-destructs proximity_radius 5.0 Distance at which the projectile detonates near its target (not direct hit)

Behavior: Plasma projectiles track their designated target using smooth interpolation (slerp) toward the target's current position, with a turn rate of approximately 6 radians/second. This gives them slight homing capability while maintaining visible flight paths that skilled pilots can dodge.

When a plasma projectile enters the proximity radius of its target, it detonates immediately, dealing damage to anything within range. If it collides directly with a body or shield area, it also detonates on contact. The projectile ignores collisions with its source ship and all descendants (preventing self-damage).

docs.title: Rocket Weapons — Homing Missiles with Explosions
docs.text:
Rocket weapons fire guided missiles that actively track their targets using proportional navigation. Rockets combine the homing capability of plasma projectiles with explosive warheads that create visible detonation effects on impact.

Tactical Role: Area denial, lock-and-forget engagements, high damage with visual impact.

Property Default Description speed 120.0 Missile cruise speed (slower than plasma but highly maneuverable) turn_rate 8.0 Maximum turning rate for homing guidance (radians/second) — higher = tighter turns damage 20.0 Damage dealt on impact or proximity detonation max_lifetime 6.0 Maximum flight time before fuel exhaustion and self-destruction proximity_radius 10.0 Distance at which the missile detonates near its target

Explosion Effects: On impact, rockets spawn a visual explosion effect: | Property | Default | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | explosion_color | Orange #FF9933 | Color of the explosion sphere | | explosion_size | 3.0 | Maximum scale multiplier for the explosion visual | | explosion_duration | 0.25 | Seconds for the explosion to expand and fade |

Homing Behavior: Rockets use smooth directional interpolation (slerp) toward their target, with the turn rate clamped per-frame to prevent instant direction changes. This creates realistic missile flight paths that can be countered by countermeasures or extreme maneuvering.

Rockets detect when they've lost their target (target nullified or out of range) and continue on their last known trajectory until fuel exhaustion or impact with any solid object. They also detonate on direct collision with any body or area that isn't part of the launching ship's hierarchy.

Shot Down Capability: Rockets can be intercepted by point defense systems or other anti-missile countermeasures via the shot_down(source) method, which triggers an explosion at the rocket's current position without dealing damage to anything else.

docs.title: Probe Weapons — Utility Scanning Probes
docs.text:
Probe weapons fire non-lethal scanning probes designed for reconnaissance, surveying, and data collection rather than combat. These probes follow their targets but emit a probe_hit signal instead of dealing damage, making them useful for discovery mechanics and environmental scanning.

Tactical Role: Non-combat utility, target identification, exploration support.

Property Default Description speed 120.0 Probe travel speed (game units/second) max_lifetime 6.0 Maximum flight time before the probe self-destructs proximity_hit_radius 3.0 Distance at which the probe registers a hit on its target

Behavior: Probes use gentle homing with a turn rate of approximately 2 radians/second (much slower than rockets), creating slow, deliberate flight paths suitable for scanning operations. When a probe enters proximity range of its target or collides with any object, it emits a probe_hit(target) signal and self-destructs without dealing damage.

The dynamic hit radius scales with speed and delta time (max(proximity_hit_radius, speed × delta × 1.25)) to prevent tunneling through small targets at high velocities. Probes ignore collisions with their source ship and all descendants.

docs.title: Countermeasures
docs.text: Countermeasure systems provide defensive capabilities against incoming threats. They are installed in dedicated countermeasure slots and can operate automatically or manually, depending on configuration.

docs.title: Point Defense Systems
docs.text:
Point defense (PD) is the most common countermeasure type, providing automated interception of incoming projectiles and rockets. PD systems detect threats within their trigger range and fire back to destroy them before they reach the ship.

Property Default Description countermeasure_type "point_defense" Classification identifying this as a point defense system auto_trigger true Whether the system activates automatically when threats are detected trigger_range 300.0 Maximum distance at which PD can detect and engage incoming projectiles cooldown 1.0 Seconds between PD activation cycles ammo_capacity 500 Total ammunition available before reloading is required ammo_per_shot 1 Ammunition consumed per interception attempt

Point defense systems are particularly effective against rocket weapons due to the rockets' relatively slow speed and predictable flight paths. They are less effective against lasers (which travel instantly) and plasma projectiles (which travel faster than most PD reaction times).

docs.title: Enemy Point Defense Gating
docs.text:
Enemy ships don't all carry point defense — it's gated by difficulty level and faction affiliation: | Property | Default | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | point_defense_chance | 0.0 | Probability that an enemy ship carries PD (0–1 scale) | | point_defense_min_difficulty | 1 | Minimum difficulty level for PD availability | | point_defense_max_difficulty | 10 | Maximum difficulty level for PD availability | | point_defense_allowed_faction_ids | [] | Faction whitelist; empty = all factions can have PD |

This means low-difficulty enemies may lack point defense entirely, while high-difficulty faction-specific enemies might only carry PD if they're on the allowed list.

docs.title: Weapon Groups
docs.text: Weapons are organized into groups of up to 8 configurable slots (WEAPON_GROUP_COUNT = 8). Each group contains one or more weapons that can be fired together or in sequence, and players can switch between groups during combat using weapon group switching mechanics.

docs.title: Fire Policies
docs.text:
Each weapon group has a fire policy that determines how its weapons are discharged:

Policy Code Description Simultaneous SIMULTANEOUS (0) All weapons in the group fire at the same time — maximum damage output but generates heat from all weapons simultaneously Sequential SEQUENTIAL (1) Weapons fire one after another in order — lower peak heat generation but reduced overall DPS

docs.title: Group Management
docs.text:
Weapon groups are managed through ShipData methods: - get_weapon_group_weapons(index) — Returns the list of weapons in a group - set_weapon_group_weapons(index, weapons) — Assigns weapons to a group - get_active_weapon_data() — Returns the primary weapon from the currently active group - set_active_weapon_group(index) — Switches to a different weapon group

The game tracks which groups are non-empty (get_non_empty_weapon_group_indices) and provides cycling through available groups (get_next_non_empty_group_index), allowing players to switch between loadouts using simple forward/backward navigation.

docs.title: Hardpoint Slots
docs.text: Weapons are physically mounted in hardpoint slots defined by ShipSlotDefinition resources with slot_type = HARDPOINT. Each hardpoint has an ID (e.g., "hardpoint_0", "hardpoint_1") and optional tags for filtering compatible weapons. The loadout system validates that only WeaponData instances are installed in hardpoint slots.

docs.title: Key Files Reference
docs.text: File Purpose Lines scripts/resources/weapon_data.gd Weapon definition: damage, heat management, beam properties, homing, charged shots 56 scripts/resources/weapon_group_data.gd Weapon group: display name, weapons array, fire policy (SIMULTANEOUS/SEQUENTIAL) 13 scripts/weapons/laser_beam.gd Laser beam visual: cylinder mesh, shader parameters, split beams, pulse effects 114 scripts/weapons/plasma_projectile.gd Plasma projectile: Area3D with travel time, proximity detonation, target tracking 192 scripts/weapons/rocket_projectile.gd Rocket missile: homing guidance, explosion effects, shot-down capability 324 scripts/weapons/probe_projectile.gd Utility probe: non-lethal scanning, probe_hit signal emission 117 scripts/resources/countermeasure_data.gd Countermeasure definition: type, auto-trigger, range, cooldown, ammo 12

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by combat-system for damage calculation, heat management, and targeting logic
  • Integrated with ships-parts-lore through hardpoint slot installation and weapon group configuration
  • Countermeasures interact with vfx-visual-effects for explosion and interception effects
  • Probe weapons feed into discovery-exploration mechanics for target identification and scanning

docs.title: World Generation Pipeline — Galaxy → Systems → Objects
Overview
docs.text:
The procedural world generation system creates an entire galaxy from a single seed, descending through four levels of abstraction: Galaxy (global bounds and density) → Systems (individual star systems with deterministic content) → Objects (3D meshes placed in the scene). Every level is fully deterministic — the same seed always produces identical results.

The pipeline follows this flow: 

GalaxyData (seed, bounds, density curve)
    ↓ generates grid coordinates
GalaxySystemData[] (one per grid cell that passes density check)
    ↓ SystemGenerator.generate_system_content()
SystemRecipe (planets, stations, enemies, POIs, megastructures)
    ↓ ProceduralLevelBuilder.build_level()
3D scene graph (MeshInstance3D, GPUParticles3D, OmniLight3D, etc.)

docs.title: GalaxyData — Global Configuration
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/resources/galaxy_data.gd (244 lines), GalaxyData is a Resource that defines the entire galaxy's structure. It serves as the root configuration for all procedural generation.

docs.title: Core Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description galaxy_seed int 12345 Master seed — same value = identical galaxy galaxy_name String "Milky Way" Display name galaxy_bounds Vector3i (10,10,10) Grid dimensions → 10×10×10 = 1000 possible system locations system_spacing float 100.0 Distance between grid points in light-years system_position_jitter float 0.35 Per-axis offset as fraction of spacing (±35%) galaxy_warp_strength float 0.65 Large-scale warp amount (0 = rigid cube, 1 = full twist/noise/disk) connectivity_radius float 150.0 Max jump drive range in light-years min_systems int 100 Minimum systems to generate

docs.title: Galaxy Warp System (_apply_large_scale_warp)
docs.text:
Transforms a rigid grid into an organic galactic shape through four sequential operations:

base_pos = Vector3(coords) * system_spacing
    ↓
1. Disk flattening — Y-axis compressed toward center using galaxy_disk_vertical_scale (0.55 default)
2. Radial twist — XZ plane rotated by normalized_radius × galaxy_warp_twist_degrees (65° default)
3. Arm banding — sin(angle × arm_count + radius × 8) pushes positions radially (arm_amplitude = 0.45, arm_count = 4)
4. Noise displacement — Three independent FastNoiseLite instances (SIMPLEX type) add organic variation

The warp is applied per-coordinate and cached in _world_position_cache for performance. Jitter is added after warping using a deterministic RNG seeded from get_system_seed(coords).

docs.title: Density Curve
docs.text: A Curve resource where X = normalized distance from center (0–1) and Y = probability multiplier. Default curve: [0, 1.2] → [0.5, 1.0] → [1.0, 0.8], creating slightly denser core and sparser edges. During generation, each grid cell's density is sampled at its normalized distance; if the random value falls below the curve sample, a system is created there.

docs.title: Deterministic Seeding
docs.text:
func get_system_seed(coords: Vector3i) -> int:
    var hash_input = "%d,%d,%d,%d" % [coords.x, coords.y, coords.z, galaxy_seed]
    return hash(hash_input)
This ensures every system gets a unique but reproducible seed derived from its grid position and the master galaxy seed.

docs.title: Coordinate Conversion
docs.text: Method Purpose coordinate_to_world(coords) Grid → world position (with warp + jitter, cached) coordinate_to_raw_world(coords) Grid → raw world position (spacing only, no warp — for density sampling) world_to_coordinate(world_pos) World → nearest grid point is_coordinate_valid(coords) Bounds check

docs.title: GalaxySystemData — Per-System Metadata
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/resources/galaxy_system_data.gd (384 lines), this lightweight resource (~100 bytes per system) stores all data needed to deterministically generate a system's content. The actual 3D objects are generated on-demand from the seed, not stored directly.

docs.title: Star Types
docs.text:
enum StarType {
    O, B, A, F, G, K, M,           # Main sequence (blue giant → red dwarf)
    WHITE_DWARF, NEUTRON, BLACK_HOLE,  # Stellar remnants
    DARK_STAR                         # Hypothetical dark matter star
}

docs.title: Economy Types
docs.text:
enum EconomyType {
    NONE, AGRICULTURAL, EXTRACTION, REFINERY,
    INDUSTRIAL, HIGH_TECH, TOURISM, SERVICE, MILITARY, PRISON
}

docs.title: Population Scale (logarithmic)
docs.text: Value Description 0 Uninhabited 1–3 Outpost → Settlement → Colony 4–6 Town → City → Metropolis 7–10 Millions → Tens of M → Hundreds of M → Billions

docs.title: Name Generation
docs.text: Three deterministic naming schemes based on the system seed: 1. Greek prefix + constellation — "Alpha Centauri", "Beta Cygni" (24 prefixes × 20 constellations) 2. Catalog number — "HIP 12345", "HD 67890" (5 catalog prefixes, 1000–99999 range) 3. Constellation + suffix — "Eridani Prime", "Lyrae Secundus" (20 constellations × 20 suffixes)

docs.title: Serialization
docs.text: Full to_dictionary() / from_dictionary() support for save file persistence, including discovered/scanned/visited flags and handcrafted system overrides.

docs.title: SystemGenerator — Content Generation Engine
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/level/system_generator.gd (3192 lines), this RefCounted class is the core generation engine. It takes a GalaxySystemData + GalaxyData and produces a complete SystemRecipe.

docs.title: Generation Pipeline
docs.text:
func generate_system_content(system_data: GalaxySystemData, galaxy_data: GalaxyData) -> SystemRecipe:
    1. Create RNG from system_data.system_seed
    2. Get faction data for this system (via ProceduralFactionAssigner)
    3. Calculate effective security (border zone effects)
    4. Create recipe from system data
    5. Generate star properties (+ optional binary companion, 0.1% chance)
    6. Generate planets (2–8 based on star type and habitable zone)
    7. Generate asteroid belts (0–2, avoiding planet orbits)
    8. Generate nebulae
    9. Generate stations (if population > 0, faction-prefixed)
    10. Generate enemy spawns (if effective_security < 0.5)
    11. Generate POIs
    12. Generate megastructures

docs.title: Planet Generation (_generate_planets)
docs.text:
Creates 2–8 planets with increasing orbital distances: - Inner planets (i=0): 0.45–0.95 AU - Second planet (i=1): 1.15–2.35 AU - Outer planets (i≥2): 2.2 + i × (2.8–8.8) AU, capped at 30 AU

Planet type is determined by orbit position relative to the star's habitable zone: | Type | Orbit Range | Radius (Earth radii) | Atmosphere | |------|-------------|---------------------|------------| | LAVA | Very close | 0.8–1.3 | Toxic (30% chance) | | ROCKY | Habitable zone | 0.5–1.5 | Standard/Thin (60% chance) | | OCEAN | Inner habitable | 0.9–1.8 | Standard (always) | | DESERT | Near habitable | 0.7–1.2 | Thin (50% chance) | | TOXIC | Outer hot | 0.9–1.4 | Toxic (always) | | GAS_GIANT | Far out | 3.0–11.0 | Thick (always), 40% ring chance, 2–8 moons | | ICE | Very far | 0.8–2.0 | Thin (40% chance), 1–2 moons |

Each planet gets: surface gravity (mass/radius²), surface temperature (orbit + star temp + atmosphere), orbital period (Kepler's third law: √(AU³) × 365.25 days), rotation period, axial tilt, resource deposits, and a 10% chance of being inhabited if in the habitable zone.

docs.title: Asteroid Belt Generation (_generate_asteroid_belts)
docs.text: Creates 0–2 belts with collision avoidance: - Radial width: 0.35–1.2 AU - Thickness: 0.08–0.25 AU - Orbit: 1.8–38 AU, checked against all planets (min gap = max(0.7, belt_width × 0.5 + 0.3)) and other belts - Density: 0.4–0.95

docs.title: Binary Star System (_generate_binary_star_data)
docs.text: 0.1% chance per system. Companion star type is weighted toward M-dwarfs (94%) with rare O/B/A types. Companion mass/radius/luminosity are scaled relative to the primary. Minimum planet orbit is increased to binary_separation × 1.8 + 0.2 AU for stability.

docs.title: Station Generation (_generate_faction_stations)
docs.text: Only generated if system population > 0. Station count and type are influenced by faction preferences, economy type, and effective security level.

docs.title: Enemy Spawn Generation (_generate_faction_enemies)
docs.text: Generated when effective_security < 0.5. Enemy presence scales inversely with security — lower security means more enemies. Faction affiliation determines enemy ship types and loadouts.

docs.title: StarGenerator — 3D Star Visuals
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/level/star_generator.gd (1085 lines), this Node creates the visual representation of stars including: - Regular stars (O through M): Scaled sphere with emissive material, directional + omni lights - Exotic stars: Black hole (accretion disk + event horizon), neutron star (pulsing beacon shader), dark star (particle cloud)

docs.title: Star LOD System
docs.text: Distance Effect < lod_close_distance_min (11,000 units) Full detail shell, billboard glow at full intensity < lod_medium_distance_min (42,000 units) Medium detail, reduced glow > 42,000 units Simplified sphere only

docs.title: Visual Components
docs.text:
• Core: Emissive sphere with star_core_emissive.gdshader
  • Detail shell: Outer layer with star_detail_shell.gdshader (animated noise)
  • Billboard glow: 2D sprite facing camera with star_billboard_glow.gdshader, fades between 7,000–18,000 units
  • Proximity lights: OmniLight3D + DirectionalLight3D scaled by star luminosity

docs.title: ProceduralLevelBuilder — 3D Scene Construction
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/level/procedural_level_builder.gd (2090 lines), this Node takes a SystemRecipe and instantiates all 3D objects into the scene graph. It's organized as sequential build phases:

docs.title: Build Order
docs.text:
1. Planets (PlanetGeneratorService) — with LOD levels, landable planet support
2. Gates (gate.tscn) — placed at GATE_RADIUS = 100,000 units from center
3. Asteroid belts (asteroid_field.gd)
4. Stations (imported GLB models with albedo tint + metallic/roughness overrides)
5. Enemy spawns (npc_ship.tscn with variant resolver)
6. POIs (ProceduralPOIBuilder — see below)
7. Megastructures (12 types, see SystemRecipe.MegastructureKind enum)

docs.title: Scale Configuration
docs.text: Parameter Default Description orbit_au_to_units 28,000 1 AU = 28,000 game units (reduced from 149.6M km for gameplay) planet_radius_units_per_earth 2,400 Earth radius in game units (raised from 50 to 150 — 3× scale) max_orbit_au 50 Maximum orbital distance for planets hero_planet_max_lod_level 16 Full detail LOD for hero planets (~800ms load) background_planet_max_lod_level 12 Reduced LOD for background planets (~100ms load)

docs.title: Megastructure Types (12 total)
docs.text:
enum MegastructureKind {
    HIGHWAY, PLANET_RING, HABITAT_RING, SHIPYARD, SOLAR_ARRAY,
    DYSON_SWARM, DYSON_SHELL_SEGMENT, AQUEDUCT, SENSOR_ARRAY,
    DEFENSE_PLATFORM, ORBITAL_TETHER, SATELLITE_CONSTELLATION
}

Each megastructure type has its own builder method (e.g., _build_planet_ring_megastructure) and dedicated script file. They're placed in a Megastructures container node under the system root.

docs.title: ProceduralPOIBuilder — POI Visual Generation
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/level/procedural_poi_builder.gd (1783 lines), this RefCounted class uses a registry pattern to generate 3D visuals for each POI type. Each POI type registers a builder function that returns a Node3D with its mesh hierarchy.

docs.title: POI Types
docs.text: Type Color Description ANOMALY Purple (0.7, 0.4, 0.9) Energy anomalies DERELICT Brown-gold (0.7, 0.6, 0.45) Abandoned ships/structures SATELLITE Blue (0.45, 0.7, 0.9) Orbital satellites RESOURCE_CACHE Green (0.4, 0.85, 0.5) Mined resource deposits SCIENTIFIC Cyan-blue (0.35, 0.75, 0.9) Research stations NAVIGATIONAL Gold (0.9, 0.8, 0.4) Navigation beacons with animated rings WARNING Red (0.9, 0.35, 0.35) Hazard warnings

docs.title: Shared Resources
docs.text:
• Meshes: Pre-allocated PrimitiveMesh instances shared across all POI instances of the same type
  • Materials: One StandardMaterial3D per POI type, cached at init time
  • Builder registry: Dictionary mapping POIType enum → callable(seed) → Node3D

docs.title: Animated Navigational Beacons
docs.text: NAVIGATIONAL POIs feature a three-ring animation system with sequential pulse effect: each ring pulses in sequence (1.2s offset), creating a "chasing light" effect around the beacon crown. The animation is implemented via an inline GDScript attached at build time.

docs.title: ProceduralFactionAssigner — Territory Assignment
docs.text:
Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/level/procedural_faction_assigner.gd (35 lines), this RefCounted class provides O(1) faction lookup using a pre-computed coordinate hash map. Territories are computed once from the galaxy seed and stored as {Vector3i → faction_id} mappings.

func configure_from_territories(territories: Array) -> void:
    for territory in territories:
        for coords in territory.system_coords:
            _coords_to_faction[coords] = territory.faction_id

docs.title: GalaxyHierarchy — Spatial Partitioning
docs.text:
Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/level/galaxy_hierarchy.gd (108 lines), this RefCounted class provides utility helpers for converting system coordinates into hierarchical IDs:

system_coords → sector_coords → quadrant_coords → region_coords
     ↓                ↓               ↓              ↓
  "sector:x,y,z"  "quadrant:x,y,z"  "region:x,y,z"

Each level uses configurable sizes from GalaxyHierarchyConfig: - sector_size — systems per sector (X, Y, Z) - sectors_per_quadrant — sectors per quadrant - quadrants_per_region — quadrants per region

ID parsing is supported via try_parse_*_id() methods for reverse lookups.

docs.title: Data Flow Summary
docs.text:
GalaxyData (seed=12345, bounds=(10,10,10))
    │
    ├─→ ProceduralFactionAssigner.configure_from_territories()  [pre-computed territories]
    │
    └─→ For each valid grid cell:
            │
            ├─→ GalaxyData.get_system_seed(coords) → system_seed
            │
            ├─→ GalaxySystemData.new() with coords, seed, star_type, economy, security
            │
            └─→ SystemGenerator.generate_system_content(system_data, galaxy_data):
                    │
                    ├─→ StarData.from_star_type(star_type) + optional binary companion
                    ├─→ _generate_planets() → 2–8 planets with types, orbits, moons
                    ├─→ _generate_asteroid_belts() → 0–2 belts
                    ├─→ _generate_nebulae() → nebula data
                    ├─→ _generate_faction_stations() → stations (if populated)
                    ├─→ _generate_faction_enemies() → enemies (if low security)
                    ├─→ _generate_pois() → points of interest
                    └─→ _generate_megastructures() → megastructure data
                            │
                            ↓ SystemRecipe
                    ProceduralLevelBuilder.build_level(recipe, root_node):
                        1. build_planets() — PlanetGeneratorService with LOD levels
                        2. build_gates() — gate.tscn at GATE_RADIUS
                        3. build_asteroid_belts() — asteroid_field.gd instances
                        4. build_stations() — imported GLB models
                        5. build_enemies() — npc_ship.tscn with variant resolver
                        6. build_pois() — ProceduralPOIBuilder registry
                        7. build_megastructures() — 12 megastructure types

docs.title: Key Files Reference
docs.text: File Purpose Lines scripts/resources/galaxy_data.gd Galaxy-level config: bounds, density curve, warp parameters, coordinate conversion 244 scripts/resources/galaxy_system_data.gd Per-system metadata: coordinates, seed, star type, economy, name generation 384 scripts/level/system_generator.gd Core generation engine: planets, asteroids, stations, enemies, POIs, megastructures 3192 scripts/level/star_generator.gd 3D star visuals: regular + exotic types, LOD, billboard glow, shaders 1085 scripts/level/procedural_level_builder.gd Scene construction from SystemRecipe: planets, gates, stations, POIs, megastructures 2090 scripts/level/procedural_faction_assigner.gd O(1) faction territory lookup via coordinate hash map 35 scripts/level/procedural_poi_builder.gd Registry-based POI visual generation with shared meshes/materials 1783 scripts/level/galaxy_hierarchy.gd Spatial partitioning: sector/quadrant/region coordinate conversion and ID management 108

docs.title: ChatService
docs.text: Networked chat system for multiplayer communication between players and NPCs.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: ChatService (scripts/services/chat_service.gd) provides real-time text messaging across the multiplayer session. It handles peer-to-peer message relay, message history, and integration with the ChatWidget UI.

docs.title: Connection Lifecycle
docs.text:
• Enables _process(true) on connect (lines 47, 77) — polls every frame while connected
  • Calls _peer.poll() + stale-peer iteration every frame
  • Supports up to 64 concurrent clients

docs.title: Message Flow
docs.text:
Client → ChatService._send_message() → _relay_to_peers() → all_connected_clients

Each message triggers _send_packet_to() for every peer with individual error logging.

docs.title: Message History
docs.text:
• _message_history stores recent messages for replay on new client connect
  • HISTORY_MAX limits history size (default: 50 messages)
  • On auth, sends full duplicate copy to new client (_message_history.duplicate())

docs.title: HIGH — Excessive _process() overhead
docs.text:
Enables _process(true) on connect. Calls _peer.poll() + stale-peer iteration every frame with up to 64 clients = significant per-frame cost.

Fix: Replace printerr with FW_Debug.debug(). Consider idle polling (timer-based instead of per-frame).

docs.title: HIGH — O(n) stale-peer scan
docs.text:
Iterates ALL peers every frame to check for disconnections. With 64 clients, this is 64 dict lookups/tick.

Fix: Use timer-based check (every 5–10s) instead of per-frame iteration.

docs.title: HIGH — No rate limiting on relay
docs.text:
Every message triggers _send_packet_to() for every peer with individual printerr. No rate limiting.

Fix: Add rate limiter (max 30 relays/sec). Remove per-send printerr.

docs.title: CRITICAL — History overflow race
docs.text:
_message_history.append(msg)          # append first (line 281)
if _message_history.size() > HISTORY_MAX:  # check after (line 282)
    _message_history.pop_front()      # trim (line 283)

History can grow to HISTORY_MAX + 1. Also _message_history.duplicate() at line 212 sends full copy on every new client auth.

Fix: Enforce limit before append: check size, pop if needed, then append.

docs.title: Integration
docs.text:
• UI: ChatWidget (scripts/ui/chat_widget.gd) — displays messages, input field
  • Network: Uses Godot's ENetMultiplayerPeer for transport
  • Autoload: Accessed globally as ChatService singleton

docs.title: Prioritized Fixes
docs.text:
Priority Action Effort Impact P0 Replace _process() with timer-based polling (5–10s interval) 30m High — eliminates per-frame overhead P0 Enforce HISTORY_MAX before append, not after 5m Medium — prevents overflow P1 Add rate limiter (max 30 relays/sec) 1h Medium — bounds network traffic P2 Replace printerr with FW_Debug.debug() 15m Low — reduces log spam

Page created: 2026-05-25 | Source: scripts/services/chat_service.gd

docs.title: CombatTargetingSolver
docs.text: Intercept point quadratic solver for combat targeting calculations.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: CombatTargetingSolver (scripts/systems/combat_targeting_solver.gd) calculates intercept points for weapons by solving a quadratic equation that accounts for target velocity, weapon speed, and distance.

docs.title: Quadratic Solver
docs.text:
Given:
  - Target position (P_t)
  - Target velocity (V_t)  
  - Weapon speed (v_w)
  - Current distance (d)

Solve for time t where:
  |P_t + V_t × t| = v_w × t

This yields a quadratic equation in t, solved via standard formula.

docs.title: Key Methods
docs.text:
• solve_intercept(target_position, target_velocity, weapon_speed) → returns intercept point
  • predict_target_position(target, time_to_impact) → projected position at impact
  • calculate_lead_angle(target, weapon_speed) → aiming offset for moving targets

docs.title: Integration
docs.text:
• Called by: TargetingManager, ShipWeapons
  • Physics: Uses SharedFlightModel deterministic state
  • Multiplayer: Server-authoritative calculations, client-side prediction via PredictionService

docs.title: Performance Notes
docs.text:
• Runs every combat tick (physics frame) for active targets
  • Quadratic solver is O(1) per target — efficient even with many NPCs
  • No .call() usage — direct typed calls preferred

Page created: 2026-05-25 | Source: scripts/systems/combat_targeting_solver.gd

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: DiscoveryAtlasCard is a UI component that displays discovered entities in an atlas/card layout. It extends Button and provides a compact visual representation of discovery data including type badges, title/subtitle labels, chip tags, and summary text. Used within the DiscoveryJournal's card-based panels (Staged, First Claims, Validated).

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The class handles card display operations within the game world, providing both runtime functionality and configuration interfaces for other systems to interact with.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: DiscoveryJournal is a persistent log of all discoveries organized by date/category. It extends Popup and provides four panel views: Staged, First Claims, Validated, and Public Codex. Cross-references with feed events and allows filtering and searching through discovery history. Integrates with DM for data retrieval and PlanetScienceDossier for detailed planetary records.

docs.title: DM (DiscoveryManager)
docs.text: File: scripts/systems/discovery_manager.gd (4654 lines) Resource: scripts/resources/discovery_facet_registry.gd — DiscoveryFacetRegistry definition table

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
DM is the core discovery and exploration system for 3dSpace Phase 6. It manages the discovery bubble, tracks all player discoveries, handles probe resources, and implements a facet-based subject discovery architecture that replaces flat entity-level scanning with structured, sellable facet data per subject (system, star, planet, moon, POI, asteroid belt, anomaly).

It operates as part of the broader FW_ framework architecture and integrates with multiple subsystems including project-architecture, fw-framework-addon.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: DM provides both runtime discovery functionality and configuration interfaces for other systems to interact with. Key responsibilities: - Discovery bubble management — auto-scan on system entry, pulse scan on demand - Subject-based facet registry — structured discovery data per celestial body/POI type - Economy bundle scanning — batch sell unsold discoveries through TS at stations - Scan persistence — sectorized save/load (v3 schema) with sold-only durable + local unsold cache - Multiplayer sync — RPC channels for discovery registration, scan updates, atlas pages

docs.title: Facet Registry System
docs.text:
DiscoveryFacetRegistry defines a static table of facet definitions keyed by subject_kind:

Subject Kind Example Facets Domain system system.overview, system.first_entry exploration star star.basic_scan, star.spectroscopy, star.activity science planet / moon body.basic_scan, body.composition, body.atmosphere, body.geology, body.radiation, body.magnetosphere, body.landing_site, body.subsurface, body.weather, body.cryo, body.demographics exploration/science poi poi.discovery exploration landmark poi.surface_record, poi.artifact_analysis surface_poi asteroid_belt belt.discovery, belt.composition mining anomaly anomaly.discovery, anomaly.analysis anomaly

Each facet has: facet_key, subject_kind, display_name, domain, base_value, requires_visit, source_methods, newsworthy, sort_index.

docs.title: Subject Hierarchy
docs.text:
system_coords -> DiscoverySubjectData (per celestial body)
    -> player_id -> subject_id -> facet_key -> DiscoveryPlayerFacetState
        -> status ("staged"/"sold"), payload, captured_at, updated_at

Published facets are tracked in _published_facets_by_subject for economy bundle selling.

docs.title: Save Schema v3
docs.text:
• v1: flat array of discoveries
  • v2: sectorized (8×8×8 system sectors)
  • v3 (current): sold-only durable discoveries + local unsold cache (_private_runtime_discoveries)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Godot autoload singleton that manages faction reputation and standing across the game world. Provides a global reputation system where player actions affect standing with each faction, which in turn influences NPC behavior, territorial control, and mission availability.

Class name: FM Base class: Node Location: scripts/autoload/faction_manager.gd

docs.title: Key Responsibilities
docs.text:
1. Reputation tracking — Per-faction float scores that change based on player actions
  2. Standing calculation — Converts reputation scores into integer standing levels (hostile, neutral, friendly, etc.)
  3. Event broadcasting — Emits signals when reputation or standing changes so other systems can react

docs.title: Signals
docs.text:
• reputation_changed(faction_id: String, new_score: float, delta: float) — Fired whenever a faction's raw reputation score changes
  • standing_changed(faction_id: String, new_standing: int) — Fired when the computed standing level changes (threshold crossing)
  • reputation_event(...) — Emitted for specific reputation-modifying events

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by npc-simulation-service for NPC faction assignment and behavior tuning
  • Consumed by procedural-level-builder for territorial allocation and faction ship spawning
  • Consumed by faction-data resource definitions (each faction has a unique ID)
  • Referenced by patrol-manager for wing faction assignment

docs.title: Known Patterns
docs.text:
• Reputation changes are typically batched — multiple small actions accumulate before triggering standing recalculation
  • Standing thresholds are defined in FactionData resources, not hardcoded

docs.title: Feed Event Service (FES)
docs.text: File: scripts/systems/feed_event_service.gd (1272 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
FES is the central event bus for the game's news feed system. It records discovery publications, faction reputation swings, and route events (delivery, mining, pirate activity), then writes them to SQLite via ServerDatabase into feed_event_log and feed_story_candidates tables. Each event is assigned a story_weight, headline_hint, and summary_hint for downstream story generation.

FES runs as an autoload registered in project.godot. It is consumed by DiscoveryManager, FactionManager, and NpcSimulationService.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The class handles feed event operations within the game world, providing both runtime functionality and configuration interfaces for other systems to interact with.

docs.title: Key Files
docs.text: • scripts/systems/feed_event_service.gd — primary implementation (1272 lines)

docs.title: Event Types
docs.text: FES recognizes three top-level categories:

docs.title: Discovery Events
docs.text:
• discovery_facet_published — First publication of a discovery facet by a player
  • discovery_facet_validated — Subsequent validation of an already-published facet

docs.title: Faction Reputation Events
docs.text: • faction_reputation_swing — Player standing change with a faction (delta-based or threshold-crossing)

docs.title: Route Events
docs.text:
• delivery — Freight delivery to a system
  • mining_delivery — Ore convoy return from local extraction
  • pirate_pressure — Pirate interception activity in a system
  • pirate_eliminated — Patrol action eliminating pirates
  • patrol_response — Dispatch of patrol response
  • trader_destroyed — Shipping loss event

docs.title: World-State Events
docs.text:
• control_shift — Faction control change in a system
  • border_pressure_spike — Border security degradation
  • frontier_census — Census survey completion

docs.title: Key Methods
docs.text:
• record_discovery_publication(event: Dictionary) -> Dictionary (line 20) — Records a discovery publication or validation, computes story metadata, writes to both feed_event_log and feed_story_candidates, upserts system directory entry
  • record_faction_event(event: Dictionary) -> Dictionary (line 235) — Records faction reputation swing with delta threshold filtering (<0.2 ignored unless standing changed)
  • record_route_event(event: Dictionary) -> Dictionary (line 355) — Records route events (delivery, mining, pirate activity) with category/story_kind resolution
  • record_world_state_event(event: Dictionary) -> Dictionary (line 520) — Records world-state events (control shifts, border pressure, frontier census)
  • rebuild_daily_rollup(date_text: String) -> Dictionary (line 970) — Rebuilds discovery and route rollups for a given date
  • build_story_candidates_for_date(date_text: String) -> Dictionary (line 979) — Regenerates story candidates from event log
  • record_system_first_discovery(event: Dictionary) -> Dictionary (line 1006) — Records first entry into a system (weight=80, milestone category)
  • upsert_system_directory_entry(system_coords: Vector3i, payload: Dictionary) (line 609) — Maintains the feed_system_directory table with system metadata

docs.title: Internal Helpers
docs.text:
• _resolve_discovery_category() — Maps facet definitions to categories: science, economy, exploration
  • _compute_discovery_story_weight() — Scores events on newsworthiness, first publication, validation count, named entities
  • _build_discovery_headline_hint() / _build_discovery_summary_hint() — Template-based headline and summary generation with fallback labels
  • _resolve_route_category() / _resolve_route_story_kind() — Route event classification into economy/security categories

docs.title: Database Schema Integration
docs.text:
FES writes to three SQLite tables:

  1. feed_event_log — Canonical event records with full metadata, JSON fields for context/payload/tags/metadata
  2. feed_story_candidates — Prioritized story candidates derived from events, used by the feed pipeline
  3. feed_system_directory — System-level directory entries updated on discovery/route events

docs.title: Story Weight Computation
docs.text:
Discovery events start at base weight 40 and add: - +25 if newsworthy (per facet definition) - +20 for first publication - +min(10, validation_count) for additional validations - +5 each for named system/subject - +5 for science category

Route events start at base 45 and add event-type bonuses (delivery: +10, pirate_pressure: +28, etc.) plus risk/cargo scaling.

docs.title: Rollup System
docs.text: After recording discovery or route events, FES triggers _rebuild_discovery_rollup_for_date() or _rebuild_route_rollup_for_date(), which query the event log for daily aggregates and write to feed_daily_rollups. This includes top systems by event count and top players.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by DiscoveryManager for discovery publication recording
  • Consumed by FactionManager for reputation swing events
  • Consumed by NpcSimulationService for NPC-driven route events
  • Writes to ServerDatabase for persistence
