docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The FlightController is the core player ship movement and physics system. It extends Godot's CharacterBody3D and handles all aspects of ship control — thrust, rotation, banking, boost, travel mode, space highway lane following, weapon firing, targeting, and multiplayer state synchronization.

Class name: FlightController Base class: CharacterBody3D Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/flight_controller.gd (3,348 lines)

docs.title: Exported Tuning Parameters
docs.text:
• base_speed: float = 40.0 — Base forward speed in world units/sec
  • boost_multiplier: float = 2.0 — Speed multiplier when boosting
  • rotation_speed: Vector3(1.6, 1.4, 2.0) — Pitch/Yaw/Roll rotation rates
  • throttle_change_rate: float = 1.5 — How quickly throttle ramps up/down
  • damping: float = 2.5 — Velocity lerp factor (assist mode inertia)
  • brake_strength: float = 3.0 — Deceleration rate when braking

docs.title: Flight Assist Modes
docs.text:
• Assist ON (default): Smooth velocity interpolation with idle throttle, capped speed
  • Assist OFF: Direct acceleration model with higher rotation/thrust multipliers, no speed cap (except boost), drift damping configurable

docs.title: View Modes
docs.text:
enum ViewMode { THIRD_PERSON, COCKPIT }
Toggle with flight_cockpit_toggle_view input action. Cockpit view saved in config.

docs.title: Terrain Contact Solver (Moon Landing Support)
docs.text:
The FlightController integrates ShipTerrainContactSolver.gd for terrain contact detection during landing operations:

  • Activation distance: configurable via terrain_contact_activation_distance (default 200.0 world units)
  • Enter/exit clearance: hysteresis between terrain_contact_enter_clearance (5.0) and terrain_contact_exit_clearance (10.0) to prevent rapid on/off toggling
  • Lookahead: terrain_contact_lookahead_sec (default 0.2s) projects velocity forward for early contact detection
  • Collision shape: resolves a TerrainContactShape node (SphereShape3D) at runtime via _resolve_terrain_contact_shape()
  • Frame domain tracking: _terrain_contact_frame_state: FrameDomainState ensures terrain contact checks use the correct celestial frame

docs.title: Surface Reconciliation Suppression
docs.text: When the ship is in terrain contact and within SURFACE_RECONCILIATION_SUPPRESS_ALTITUDE, network reconciliation corrections are suppressed to prevent the server from fighting the local landing state. See _should_suppress_surface_reconciliation() for logic.

docs.title: Debug Support
docs.text: debug_terrain_contact: bool = true enables altitude logging, collision mode reporting, and debug visualization of contact sphere data via get_terrain_contact_sphere_data().

docs.title: Physics Model
docs.text: The flight physics is implemented as a deterministic shared simulation via SharedFlightModel.simulate(). This pure function takes ship state, input, and parameters — producing identical results on both client and server for multiplayer consistency.

docs.title: Core Simulation Loop (per _physics_process frame)
docs.text:
1. Read input axes from NetworkSnapshotGenerator.build_snapshot()
  2. Build FlightInput struct with pitch/roll/yaw/throttle/strafe/brake/boost/fire
  3. Call SharedFlightModel.simulate(ship, input, params, state, delta) — mutates ship rotation and velocity
  4. Update space highway travel (if on a lane)
  5. Sync runtime state back to FlightController fields
  6. Call move_and_slide() for collision response

docs.title: Throttle Model
docs.text:
• Assist ON: Throttle ramps 0→1 via analog axis, clamped [0,1]. Idle throttle prevents zero thrust. Brake reduces throttle directly.
  • Assist OFF: Throttle ramps -1→1 (reverse allowed). Direct acceleration: velocity += target_velocity * accel_scale * delta. No idle throttle floor.

docs.title: Boost System
docs.text:
• Boost level: 0.0 → 5.0 (configurable via _max_boost_level)
  • Consumes 1.0 unit per second while active
  • Recharges at boost_recharge_rate when not boosting
  • Locked until boost_min_threshold * max_boost_level is reached after depletion
  • Mutually exclusive with travel mode

docs.title: Travel Mode (FTL/Jump)
docs.text:
• Activated via flight_travel_mode input action
  • 0.5-second spinup progress (configurable)
  • Speed multiplier: 100× base speed during active travel
  • Consumes fuel at _travel_mode_fuel_consumption * delta per second
  • Locked for 5 seconds after taking damage (TRAVEL_MODE_DAMAGE_LOCKOUT)

docs.title: Space Highway Lane System
docs.text:
• Ships can attach to predefined SpaceHighwayLane nodes
  • Grace period (0.45s) and blend time (0.22s) for smooth lane transitions
  • Speed follows lane's travel speed × LANE_SPEED_MULTIPLIER (0.72)
  • Exit requires lateral speed > 1800 m/s or specific strafe/brake thresholds

docs.title: Runtime Roles
docs.text:
@export_enum("full", "remote_visual", "server_authority") var runtime_role: String = "full"
- full: Client-owned ship with full control, VFX, and systems - remote_visual: Visual-only replica (no weapons, no HUD, optimized particles) - server_authority: Server-side ship simulation (no local VFX or view nodes)

docs.title: Ship Systems Coordinator Integration
docs.text: The ShipSystemsCoordinator manages subsystems: shields, fuel, cargo, mining. The FlightController queries it for status info and delegates weapon firing to the current target.

docs.title: SharedFlightModel — Deterministic Physics
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/network/shared_flight_model.gd (236 lines). This is the single source of truth for flight physics: - SharedFlightModel.simulate() called by both client FlightController and server NetworkShipManager - Takes identical inputs → produces identical ship state mutations - Three inner classes: FlightState, FlightInput, FlightParams

docs.title: Client Prediction System
docs.text: Located at /home/j/3d-space/scripts/systems/prediction_service.gd. The FlightController maintains a prediction buffer for client-side input anticipation: 1. Client records predicted states with sequence numbers 2. Server sends authoritative snapshots with position, rotation, velocity 3. receive_authoritative_snapshot() evaluates if correction is needed 4. Corrections applied as one-shot offsets (not per-frame overrides) to avoid fighting move_and_slide() 5. Hard snap for first authoritative snapshot after spawn

docs.title: Network Snapshot Flow
docs.text:
• Client: get_network_snapshot() → NetworkSnapshotGenerator.build_snapshot(self) → sent to server
  • Server: apply_network_snapshot(state) → applies position/rotation/velocity from remote ships
  • Ship replication service (SRS) handles ongoing state sync for all visible ships

docs.title: Key Files Reference
docs.text: File Purpose Lines scripts/flight_controller.gd Main flight controller — movement, input, multiplayer 3,348 scripts/network/shared_flight_model.gd Deterministic physics simulation (shared client/server) 236 scripts/systems/prediction_service.gd Client-side prediction buffer and reconciliation ~200 scripts/systems/ship_systems_coordinator.gd Ship subsystem management (shields, fuel, weapons) ~150 scripts/shared/lod_config.gd FwLodConfig — camera/LOD constants used by flight 113 scripts/flight/flight_vfx_sync.gd Flight state → VFX visual sync ~80 scripts/flight/flight_local_view_mode.gd Cockpit/third-person view mode management ~60 scripts/flight/flight_debug_helpers.gd Debug panel info emission utilities ~50

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by project-architecture as the primary player interaction system
  • Uses fw-state-machine for state transitions (travel mode spinup, boost lock)
  • Integrates with combat-system via weapon firing and targeting manager
  • Multiplayer sync through networking-system — SharedFlightModel is the physics bridge

docs.title: FpsHudOverlay
docs.text: File: scripts/ui/fps_hud_overlay.gd (437 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
FpsHudOverlay is an FPS-style HUD overlay system that replaces the traditional ship HUD with instrument widgets. It provides a CanvasLayer-based UI with reticle, interact prompts, compass ring, radial menu, and science instrument widgets (lidar, magnetometer, radiation meter, spectrometer, planet science dossier). Widgets are bootstrapped via FpsHudWidgetBootstrap with priority levels.

Class name: FpsHudOverlay Base class: CanvasLayer Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/ui/fps_hud_overlay.gd (437 lines)

docs.title: Exported Visibility Toggles
docs.text:

  • show_ship_interact: bool = true — Toggle ship interaction prompts
  • show_interact_prompt: bool = true — Toggle general interact prompts
  • show_dev_readout: bool = true — Toggle developer readout panel
  • show_compass: bool = true — Toggle compass ring widget
  • show_radial_menu: bool = true — Toggle radial menu availability
  • show_spectrometer: bool = false — Toggle spectrometer widget

docs.text:

  • %PromptContainer — Interact prompt widgets
  • %DevReadoutContainer — Developer information readouts
  • %CompassRingContainer — Compass ring display
  • %RadialMenuContainer — Radial menu overlay
  • %ChatContainer — Chat widget (dynamically loaded)
  • %SpectrometerContainer — Spectrometer instrument widget
  • %RadiationMeterContainer — Radiation meter instrument widget
  • %PlanetScienceDossierContainer — Planet science dossier widget

docs.title: Public API
docs.text:

  • _activate_instrument(widget: FpsInstrumentWidget) — Activate/dismiss an instrument widget (exclusive, one at a time)
  • show_surface_notification(text: String, duration: float = 8.0, text_color: Color = Color.WHITE) — Show a surface toast notification via ToastNotification

docs.title: Widget Bootstrap Registration
docs.text: Widgets are registered with priorities during _register_widget_bootstraps(): - CRITICAL: Reticle, ShipInteract, InteractPrompt - HIGH: DevReadout - MEDIUM: CompassRing, RadialMenu, Spectrometer, RadiationMeter, PlanetScienceDossier

docs.title: FpsInstrumentWidget Base Class
docs.text: Abstract base for all instrument widgets. Provides: - activate(avatar: PlayerAvatarController) — Called when widget is activated - dismiss() — Called to dismiss the widget - dismissed signal — Emitted when dismissed - _unlocks_mouse() -> bool — Whether activation should release mouse capture

docs.title: Concrete Instrument Widgets
docs.text:
• FpsLidarWidget — Lidar scanning for terrain mapping (dynamically instantiated)
  • FpsSpectrometerWidget — Spectral analysis of surface deposits
  • FpsRadiationMeterWidget — Radiation level measurement and display
  • FpsPlanetScienceDossierWidget — Comprehensive planet science data panel

docs.title: Surface Reading Integration
docs.text: The overlay connects to PlayerAvatarController signals on avatar change: - gravity_reading -> Shows gravity toast notification - temperature_reading -> Shows temperature toast notification - surface_reading_progress -> Shows scanning progress toast - surface_reading_completed -> Shows completion toast + screen flash effect - radiation_scan_requested -> Activates radiation meter widget - spectrometer_scan_requested -> Activates spectrometer widget - planet_science_dossier_requested -> Activates dossier widget - lidar_scan_requested -> Activates lidar widget

docs.title: Radial Menu Integration
docs.text: When the avatar requests a radial menu (radial_menu_requested signal), the overlay resolves and opens the FpsRadialMenu. Entry selection triggers _on_radial_entry_selected() which calls avatar.perform_science_action(index).

docs.title: Avatar Lifecycle
docs.text: The overlay tracks the current local avatar via PlayerPossession.local_avatar_changed signal. On avatar change, it: 1. Disconnects all signals from the old avatar 2. Connects all signals to the new avatar 3. Syncs all registered widgets with the new avatar via _sync_widget_with_avatar()

docs.title: Widget Bootstrap System
docs.text: Uses FpsHudWidgetBootstrap for deferred, priority-ordered widget creation. The bootstrap runs in _process() and creates widgets asynchronously to avoid frame stalls. Dynamic widgets are started on the next process frame via CONNECT_ONE_SHOT.

docs.title: Screen Flash Effect
docs.text: On surface reading completion, a full-screen ColorRect is created with the result color, then tweened to transparent over 0.4s for visual feedback.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumes ToastNotification for surface reading notifications
  • Integrates with PlayerAvatarController for science action triggers
  • Uses FpsHudWidgetBootstrap for widget lifecycle management
  • Connects to FpsRadialMenu for science action selection

docs.title: Source References
docs.text: • scripts/ui/fps_hud_overlay.gd (437 lines)

docs.title: FwCameraService
docs.text: File: scripts/systems/fw_camera_service.gd (146 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
FwCameraService is a centralized cached Camera3D reference with change notification signal. It eliminates per-frame viewport.get_camera_3d() lookups across multiple systems by maintaining a single cached camera reference and emitting camera_changed when the active camera switches. This is especially important during disembark transitions where the active camera may switch between ship-mounted and avatar-mounted cameras.

Class name: (no class_name declaration — accessed as autoload) Base class: Node Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/systems/fw_camera_service.gd (146 lines) Registration: Autoload in project.godot as FwCameraService

docs.title: Internal State
docs.text: • _camera: Camera3D = null — Cached reference to the active viewport camera

docs.title: Signals
docs.text: • camera_changed(new_camera: Camera3D) — Emitted whenever the active camera changes (null when no camera)

docs.title: Public API
docs.text:
• get_active_camera() -> Camera3D — Get the cached camera reference (validates on call)
  • is_camera_valid() -> bool — Check if camera is non-null, instance-valid, and inside tree
  • get_camera_position() -> Vector3 — Return camera's global transform origin (ZERO if invalid)
  • get_camera_transform() -> Transform3D — Return camera's global transform (IDENTITY if invalid)
  • get_camera_forward() -> Vector3 — Return negative Z basis vector as forward direction (FORWARD if invalid)
  • get_camera_far() -> float — Return camera far clip distance (1500.0 default if invalid)

docs.title: Internal Methods
docs.text:
• _validate_camera() — Core validation: compares viewport's current camera against cached reference, emits signal on change
  • _refresh_camera() — Force cache invalidation and re-validation
  • _get_active_trace_id() -> String — Extracts active disembark trace ID from PlayerPossession for debug logging
  • _get_control_mode_label() -> String — Returns "SHIP", "LANDED_MENU", or "ON_FOOT" based on current control mode
  • _describe_possession_camera(ship_camera: bool) -> String — Describes the ship or avatar camera for debug traces

docs.title: Change Detection
docs.text: On every _process() frame, _validate_camera() calls viewport.get_camera_3d() and compares against the cached reference. If different (or if viewport became null), it updates the cache and emits camera_changed. This runs once per frame rather than having each system poll independently.

docs.title: Disembark Debug Integration
docs.text:
The service integrates with FlightHitchLog for debug tracing during disembark transitions. When the camera changes, it logs detailed trace information including: - Active disembark trace ID (from PlayerPossession) - Old and new camera paths and positions - Current control mode (SHIP/LANDED_MENU/ON_FOOT) - Ship-mounted and avatar-mounted camera descriptions

This enables diagnosing camera switching issues during the critical disembark/reenter transition period.

docs.title: Control Mode Awareness
docs.text: The service queries PlayerPossession for the current control mode to provide context in debug traces: - Mode 0 = SHIP (ship-mounted camera) - Mode 1 = LANDED_MENU (landed menu overlay) - Mode 2 = ON_FOOT (avatar/first-person camera)

docs.title: Default Values
docs.text: When no valid camera is available, getter methods return safe defaults rather than null: - Position: Vector3.ZERO - Transform: Transform3D() (identity) - Forward: Vector3.FORWARD - Far clip: 1500.0

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by FpsHudOverlay for camera-dependent HUD positioning
  • Consumed by PlayerAvatarController for avatar camera management
  • Integrates with FlightHitchLog for disembark debug tracing
  • Used as autoload: FwCameraService.get_active_camera()

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Framework configuration service — a centralized ConfigFile wrapper that provides typed access to game settings with default value registration and persistence. Used throughout the project for window preferences, graphics settings, input bindings, and framework-level configuration.

Class name: FW_ConfigService Base class: Node (autoload) Location: addons/fw/Config/FW_ConfigService.gd

docs.title: Key Responsibilities
docs.text:
1. Default registration — register_defaults() accepts a dictionary of section→key→value defaults
  2. Persistence — Loads from and saves to user://save/framework_config.cfg (configurable)
  3. Fallback resolution — If a key doesn't exist in the saved config, returns the registered default
  4. Change notification — Emits changed(section, key, value) signal when values are set

docs.title: Key Methods
docs.text:
• register_defaults(defaults_by_section: Dictionary) — Register fallback defaults
  • load(path: String = DEFAULT_PATH) -> void — Load config from file
  • save() -> void — Save current config to file
  • get_value(section, key, fallback) -> Variant — Get value with default fallback chain
  • set_value(section, key, value) — Set and persist a value

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by FW_WindowPrefs for display settings
  • Consumed by FW_InputRebindService for input binding storage
  • Referenced by graphics settings UI for live preview and persistence
  • Used by FW_SaveService as part of the save system

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Framework scene transition router — manages loading, unloading, and transitioning between game scenes. Provides a unified API for scene changes with built-in transition effects (directional wipe, iris, cross-shaped, etc.) and rotation support.

Class name: FW_SceneRouter Base class: Node (autoload) Location: addons/fw/Scenes/FW_SceneRouter.gd

docs.title: Key Responsibilities
docs.text:
1. Scene transitions — Load new scenes with animated transitions
  2. Transition presets — Built-in transition types: directional wipe, corner wipe, iris, spike, overlapping diamonds, center-clock
  3. Rotation support — Handles display rotation changes via rotation_changed signal
  4. Lifecycle tracking — Emits transition_started(path) and transition_finished(path, ok) signals

docs.title: Transition Types
docs.text:
• Type 0: Directional wipe (grid-based pixel wipe)
  • Type 2: Iris/circular transition
  • Type 3: Sector-based transition

Transition parameters are configurable per-preset: grid_size, edges, shape_feather, rotation_angle, from_center, stagger.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by FW_Bootstrap for initial scene loading
  • Referenced by main game flow for title→game transitions
  • Used by UI systems for modal overlay dismissal and scene return

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Framework state machine implementation used throughout the 3dSpace project for managing complex object lifecycles and behavior modes. Provides explicit state transitions with enter/exit/update callbacks.

Class name: FW_StateMachine Base class: (extends Node or RefCounted — needs verification) Location: addons/fw/State/FW_StateMachine.gd

docs.title: Key Responsibilities
docs.text:
1. State registration — States are added with names and callback dictionaries
  2. Explicit transitions — transition_to(state_name, data) moves between states
  3. Lifecycle callbacks — Each state can define on_enter, on_exit, on_update, etc.
  4. State isolation — State-specific logic is encapsulated in registered state definitions

docs.title: Usage Pattern (from project conventions)
docs.text:
_state_machine.add_state(&"preload", {
    "on_enter": func(_prev, _data): _start_preload()
})
_state_machine.transition_to(&"playing")

States are identified by StringName keys. Callbacks are lambda functions that receive (previous_state_name, transition_data).

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:

  • Used in ui-hud-subsystems systems for menu state and overlay management
  • Referenced by combat-system systems for weapon/fire mode states
  • See also: FW_State (supporting class)

docs.title: FW_ResourceCache
docs.text: Preload queue and resource caching system for efficient asset loading.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: FW_ResourceCache (addons/fw/Resources/FW_ResourceCache.gd) provides a centralized cache for frequently accessed resources (textures, scenes, scripts) with preload queue management.

docs.title: Cache Layers
docs.text:
1. Preload queue: Resources queued during _ready() for eager loading
  2. Runtime cache: LRU-style cache for dynamically loaded resources
  3. Reference counting: Tracks active references to prevent premature unloading

docs.title: Key Methods
docs.text:
• preload(path: StringName) → loads and caches resource immediately
  • get_cached(path: StringName) -> Resource → returns cached resource or null
  • queue_for_loading(path: StringName, priority: int) → adds to preload queue
  • evict_least_recently_used() → removes least accessed resource

docs.title: Integration with FW_PreloadQueue
docs.text: Works alongside: - FW_PreloadQueue: Manages async loading of queued resources - GraphicsRuntimeResources: Delegates heavy VFX/resource loading

docs.title: Performance Notes
docs.text:
• Cache size bounded by available memory
  • Preload queue processed during scene transitions (no frame stutters)
  • Reference counting prevents unloading of in-use resources

docs.title: GasSniffer
docs.text: File: scripts/player/gas_sniffer.gd (248 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
GasSniffer is a physics-based FPS surface instrument that detects and analyzes the atmosphere of planets and moons. It launches upward from the player's position, drifts through the atmosphere collecting altitude samples, then descends to land on the surface where it emits atmospheric composition data. The instrument is a RigidBody3D that uses buoyancy-like launch mechanics and terrain clamping for realistic physics behavior.

Class name: GasSniffer Base class: RigidBody3D Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/player/gas_sniffer.gd (248 lines)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The GasSniffer provides atmospheric analysis data including atmosphere type, gas composition percentages, surface pressure in atmospheres, atmospheric depth in kilometers, and whether the planet exhibits atmospheric layering. It is part of the FPS instrument suite accessible via the radial science menu.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text:
• atmosphere_measured(data: Dictionary, entity_id: String, display_name: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Emitted when atmospheric data is collected after landing
  • atmosphere_type (int) — Type code from the planet preset
  • atmosphere_composition (Dictionary) — Gas composition map with percentages
  • surface_pressure_atm (float) — Surface pressure in standard atmospheres
  • atmospheric_depth_km (float) — Total atmospheric depth in kilometers
  • has_layering (bool) — True if multiple altitude samples were recorded during flight

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: • configure(gravity_g: float, planet_node: Node3D, entity_id: String, display_name_val: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Sets up the instrument with gravity scaling, planet reference, and metadata. Adjusts bounce based on gravity (low-G = bouncier).

docs.title: Physics Lifecycle
docs.text:
• _launch() — Applies upward impulse after a 50ms settle delay. Direction is radial from planet center. Intensity scales as 18.0 * mass. Brightens the intake ring emission on launch.
  • _on_body_entered(_body: Node) — Triggered when the instrument lands. Waits for at least 1.5s of flight time before emitting data, then schedules self-destruction after 5 seconds.

docs.title: Data Collection
docs.text: • _emit_atmosphere_data() — Reads PlanetPreset.science_profile from the planet node to populate atmosphere composition, pressure, depth, and layering detection (multiple altitude samples during ascent/descent indicate layering).

docs.title: Movement Control
docs.text: • kick(player_pos: Vector3, horizontal_dir: Vector3, strength: float = 20.0) — Kicks the instrument horizontally from the player's position with a radial component for repositioning.

docs.title: Internal State
docs.text:
• _sample_altitudes (Array[float]) — Records altitude samples during flight; gaps >5m between consecutive samples are recorded to detect atmospheric layering
  • _despawn_timer — Auto-destructs after 120 seconds if not landed
  • _gravity_g — Gravity multiplier applied via default_gravity * _gravity_g

docs.title: Physics Integration
docs.text: The instrument uses _integrate_forces() for frame-synced physics updates. It tracks the planet body's orbital motion by applying position offsets and velocity changes, decomposing absolute velocity into body-relative + reference components. This avoids oscillation on moons with significant planet_delta.

docs.title: Terrain Clamping
docs.text: Uses planet_node.sample_height_and_normal() to prevent falling through analytical terrain. When detected below surface, repositions above the normal by 0.15 units and reflects downward velocity.

docs.title: Surface Interest Registration
docs.text: Registers with the planet node's surface interest system via set_surface_interest() and set_surface_interest_config() (patch_radius: 30.0, patch_resolution: 10) for LOD optimization.

docs.title: Visual Feedback

docs.title: GravityBall
docs.text: File: scripts/player/gravity_ball.gd (198 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
GravityBall is a physics-based FPS surface instrument that measures local gravitational field strength. It launches upward from the player's position, falls back to the surface under gravity, and after settling for 0.5 seconds emits the configured gravity value as a measurement. The name reflects its spherical shape and purpose — it literally bounces on the surface while measuring gravity.

Class name: GravityBall Base class: RigidBody3D Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/player/gravity_ball.gd (198 lines)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The GravityBall provides a simple but essential measurement: the local gravitational acceleration value in G-units. This data is used for environmental awareness, navigation planning, and planet science documentation. It is part of the FPS instrument suite accessible via the radial science menu.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text: • gravity_measured(gravity_value: float, entity_id: String, display_name: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Emitted after the ball settles for 0.5 seconds with the measured gravity value in G-units

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: • configure(gravity_g: float, planet_node: Node3D, entity_id: String, display_name_val: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Sets up the instrument with the gravity value to report and surface interest registration (patch_radius: 20.0, patch_resolution: 8). Bounce is set to 0.55 * bounce_scale where bounce_scale = clampf(1.0 / sqrt(_gravity_g), 0.4, 2.0). This means low-G bodies produce bouncier balls while high-G bodies produce more deadened ones.

docs.title: Physics Behavior
docs.text:
• Gravity force: dir * default_gravity * _gravity_g * mass — Applied toward planet center using the configured gravity multiplier
  • The ball is launched upward by the player and falls back under gravity
  • After settling (contact with surface detected), waits 0.5 seconds then emits measurement

docs.title: Data Output
docs.text: The signal carries a single float value (_gravity_g) representing the local gravitational acceleration in G-units, along with entity metadata for tracking.

docs.title: Movement Control
docs.text: • kick(player_pos: Vector3, horizontal_dir: Vector3, strength: float = 20.0) — Kicks the ball horizontally from player position. Resets _has_settled to allow re-triggering of the measurement cycle.

docs.title: Simplest FPS Instrument
docs.text: GravityBall is the simplest of all FPS instruments — it has no complex scan phases, waveform generation, or multi-step data collection. The workflow is: launch -> fall -> settle -> wait 0.5s -> emit gravity value. This makes it ideal for quick environmental checks.

docs.title: Velocity Decomposition Pattern
docs.text: Despite its simplicity, GravityBall uses the same _integrate_forces() pattern as other instruments for body-relative velocity tracking. The comments in the source explain this design: "The position offset keeps the ball aligned with the collider; the velocity change matches only the change in body velocity (not the full velocity), avoiding accumulation." This avoids both the position-offset oscillation from planet_delta on moons and the lateral-launch problem from raw frame-chain velocity.

docs.title: Bounce Scaling
docs.text: Bounce is inversely proportional to the square root of gravity: 1.0 / sqrt(_gravity_g). On a 0.25G body, bounce_scale = 2.0 (very bouncy). On a 4.0G body, bounce_scale = 0.5 (deadened). This creates realistic physics behavior across the full range of planetary gravities.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by PlayerAvatarController for instrument deployment
  • Gravity value comes from configure() parameter set by the player's current planet/moon
  • Integrates with FPS HUD via FpsHudOverlay for gravity reading display
  • Uses BodyFrameTransform for planet-relative positioning

docs.title: LidarMapper
docs.text: File: scripts/player/lidar_mapper.gd (30 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
LidarMapper is a static utility class for processing LiDAR scan results into structured terrain analysis data. Unlike the other FPS instruments which are physics-based RigidBody3D entities, LidarMapper operates as a pure computation service that takes raw sample counts and terrain metadata to produce quantified terrain characteristics including roughness, slope angles, depression counts, and ridge counts.

Class name: LidarMapper Base class: RefCounted (static utility) Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/player/lidar_mapper.gd (30 lines)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The LidarMapper converts raw LiDAR scan data into a structured dictionary of terrain metrics. It is called after the player performs a LiDAR scan to finalize and quantify the results. The output feeds into the FPS HUD widget display and planet science dossier.

docs.title: Static Method
docs.text:
• finalize(sample_count: int, terrain_data: Dictionary) -> Dictionary — Takes raw sample count and terrain metadata dictionary, returns structured analysis results with these keys:
  • terrain_roughness (float, 0.0-1.0) — Computed as (1.0 - plains_coverage) * 0.7 + ruggedness * 0.3, clamped to [0, 1]
  • terrain_max_slope_deg (float) — Maximum slope angle in degrees from terrain data
  • topographic_map_quality (int) — Equal to the sample count passed in
  • depressions_detected (int, 0-12) — Number of craters/depressions detected based on crater coverage and probability
  • ridge_count (int, 0-8) — Number of ridges computed from ruggedness (ruggedness * 8.0)

docs.title: Input Terrain Data Keys
docs.text: The terrain_data dictionary is expected to contain: - ruggedness_strength (float, default 0.5) — Overall terrain ruggedness factor - crater_coverage (float, default 0.25) — Fraction of surface covered by craters - plains_coverage (float, default 0.5) — Fraction of flat plains terrain - crater_probability (float, default 0.0) — Probability factor for crater detection - craters_enabled (bool, default false) — Whether craters are active on this body - max_slope_deg (float, default 15.0) — Maximum slope angle in degrees

docs.title: Static Design Pattern
docs.text: LidarMapper is a pure static utility with no instance state. It takes all input as parameters and returns results as a dictionary. This design allows it to be called from any context without needing an instantiated object, making it suitable for integration into the scan pipeline where terrain data is assembled asynchronously.

docs.title: Deterministic Computation
docs.text: All calculations are deterministic given the same inputs — no random number generation or time-dependent behavior. The snappedf() calls ensure consistent rounding of output values (0.01 precision for roughness, 0.1 precision for slope angles).

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Called by PlayerAvatarController after LiDAR scan completion
  • Output consumed by FpsLidarWidget for display
  • Feeds into planet science dossier data aggregation

docs.title: MagnetometerDrifter
docs.text: File: scripts/player/magnetometer_drifter.gd (293 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
MagnetometerDrifter is a physics-based FPS surface instrument that measures planetary magnetic fields through an ascending drift scan. It launches upward from the player's position and rises for 10 seconds while continuously sampling magnetic field data, emitting progress updates during ascent. The instrument uses buoyancy mechanics to float upward against gravity, with wind forces causing lateral drift for realistic sensor coverage.

Class name: MagnetometerDrifter Base class: RigidBody3D Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/player/magnetometer_drifter.gd (293 lines)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The MagnetometerDrifter provides comprehensive magnetic field analysis including field strength in Gauss, magnetosphere classification, field polarity, radiation belt presence, and magnetosphere shape. It is part of the FPS instrument suite accessible via the radial science menu.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text:
• magnetic_measured(data: Dictionary, entity_id: String, display_name: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Emitted when scan completes with full magnetic field data
  • magnetic_progress(ratio: float, field_strength: float, field_polarity: String, signal_strength: float) — Emitted every 0.5s during ascent with current progress and readings
  • magnetic_ascent_active(elapsed: float, max_duration: float) — Emitted continuously during the rising state
  • magnetic_surface_impact() — Emitted when the instrument crashes into terrain

docs.title: State Machine (enum)
docs.text:
• State.RISING — Instrument is ascending and collecting data
  • State.COMPLETED — Scan finished successfully
  • State.CRASHED — Instrument hit terrain before scan completion
  • State.DONE — Final state, no further processing

docs.title: Constants
docs.text:
• SCAN_DURATION: float = 10.0 — Total scan time in seconds
  • OUT_OF_RANGE_DEFAULT: float = 5000.0 — Default maximum distance from planet center
  • BUOYANCY_FACTOR: float = 1.2 — Buoyancy multiplier (exceeds gravity for upward drift)
  • WIND_FORCE: float = 0.08 — Lateral wind force magnitude
  • PROGRESS_INTERVAL: float = 0.5 — Time between progress signal emissions

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
• configure(gravity_g: float, planet_node: Node3D, entity_id: String, display_name_val: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Sets up the instrument. Computes _out_of_range as max of spawn_radius * 3.0 or 5000.0. Registers surface interest (patch_radius: 30.0, patch_resolution: 8).
  • set_player_pos(pos: Vector3) — Updates player position for signal strength computation

docs.title: Data Output Dictionary Keys
docs.text:
• magnetic_field_strength_gauss (float) — Field strength from planet science profile
  • magnetosphere (String) — Magnetosphere classification ("none", "dipole", etc.)
  • field_polarity (String) — Polarity direction ("normal" or reversed)
  • has_radiation_belt (bool) — Whether the planet has a radiation belt
  • magnetosphere_shape (String) — Shape of magnetosphere ("dipole" default)
  • scan_incomplete (bool) — True if scan was interrupted by crash

docs.title: Physics Behavior
docs.text:
• Weight force: dir * default_g * _gravity_g * mass (downward toward planet center)
  • Buoyancy force: -dir * default_g * _gravity_g * mass * BUOYANCY_FACTOR (upward, 1.2x weight for net ascent)
  • Wind force: perpendicular to radial direction using dir.cross(Vector3.UP).normalized() * WIND_FORCE * mass

docs.title: Visual Feedback
docs.text:
• _update_drifter_signal_visual() — Adjusts material alpha based on signal strength (0.1-1.0 range)
  • _update_drifter_glow() — Sets emission color and intensity based on field strength; pulse color shifts with field intensity
  • On completion: scales down to 0.001 over 0.5s then self-destructs

docs.title: Signal Strength Computation
docs.text: _compute_signal_strength() calculates the dot product between the drifter's direction from planet center and the player's direction, clamped to [0, 1]. This provides directional signal quality feedback based on how aligned the instrument is with the player.

docs.title: Physics Integration
docs.text: Uses _integrate_forces() for frame-synced physics updates with body-relative velocity tracking. The same pattern as other FPS instruments: position offset + velocity change decomposition to avoid oscillation on moving bodies.

docs.title: Scan Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Spawn grace period (3.0s) — No collision detection during initial spawn
  2. Rising phase — Buoyancy-driven ascent for SCAN_DURATION (10s), emitting progress every 0.5s
  3. Completion — Either successful scan or crash-triggered partial emission
  4. Out-of-range check — Aborts scan if distance from planet center exceeds _out_of_range

docs.title: Surface Interest Registration
docs.text: Registers with the planet node's surface interest system for LOD optimization (patch_radius: 30.0, patch_resolution: 8).

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by PlayerAvatarController for instrument deployment
  • Reads data from PlanetPreset.science_profile for magnetic field parameters
  • Integrates with FPS HUD via FpsHudOverlay for widget display and progress visualization
  • Uses BodyFrameTransform for planet-relative positioning

docs.title: NetworkAvatarManager
docs.text: File: scripts/network/network_avatar_manager.gd (760 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
NetworkAvatarManager manages player avatar lifecycle in multiplayer: disembark/reenter ship, spawn/despawn local and remote avatars, and snapshot replication. It maintains three avatar pools — LocalAvatars, AuthoritativeAvatars (server-side), and RemoteAvatarProxies — and handles the server-authoritative disembark flow with DisembarkSpawnResolver.

Class name: NetworkAvatarManager Base class: Node3D Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/network/network_avatar_manager.gd (760 lines) Scene: Loaded in scenes/flight/system_flight.tscn as a named node

docs.title: Constants
docs.text:
• AVATAR_PATCH_RADIUS: float = 40.0 — Collision patch rebuild radius for avatar walking
  • AVATAR_PATCH_RESOLUTION: int = 32 — Grid resolution for collision patches (~1.25m sample spacing)
  • AVATAR_PATCH_COLLISION_SKIN: float = 0.35 — Collision skin thickness for avatar terrain
  • SNAPSHOT_MAX_STEP_DISTANCE: float = 25.0 — Maximum position delta per snapshot step

docs.title: Avatar Pools (internal dictionaries)
docs.text:
• _authoritative_avatars: Dictionary — Server-side avatars keyed by peer_id
  • _remote_avatars: Dictionary — Remote player avatar proxies keyed by peer_id
  • _avatar_ships: Dictionary — Maps peer_id to their associated FlightController
  • _avatar_anchor_planets: Dictionary — Maps avatar instance_id to surface planet node
  • _avatar_anchor_configured: Dictionary — Tracks which avatars have collision patches configured

docs.title: Public API (Client -> Server RPC)
docs.text:
• request_local_player_disembark(ship: FlightController, trace_id: String = "") -> bool — Request disembark from ship (RPCs to server via AvatarReplicationService)
  • request_local_player_reenter_ship(ship: FlightController) -> bool — Request re-enter ship (validates distance <= PlayerPossession.reenter_distance)

docs.title: Server RPC Handlers
docs.text:
• handle_player_disembark_request(sender_peer_id: int, trace_id: String = "") — Server-side disembark acceptance flow
  • handle_player_reenter_request(sender_peer_id: int) — Server-side re-enter acceptance flow
  • handle_disembark_accepted(avatar_payload: Dictionary, world_instance_id: String = "") — Client receives disembark spawn payload and creates local avatar
  • handle_remote_avatar_spawn(peer_id: int, avatar_payload: Dictionary) — Spawn a remote player's avatar proxy
  • handle_remote_avatar_despawn(peer_id: int) — Despawn a remote player's avatar proxy
  • handle_avatar_snapshot_batch(snapshots: Array[Dictionary]) — Apply batched snapshot updates for remote avatars
  • handle_local_avatar_snapshot(sender_peer_id: int, snapshot: Dictionary) — Server receives local avatar snapshot (validates step distance <= 25m)

docs.title: Internal Methods
docs.text:
• _server_accept_disembark() — Full server-side disembark flow: reanchors ship pose, resolves spawn point via DisembarkSpawnResolver, spawns authoritative avatar, registers collision patch, broadcasts to peers
  • _server_accept_reenter() — Validates avatar distance to ship anchor, despawns avatar, notifies other peers
  • _spawn_avatar_instance(parent, node_name, snapshot) — Instantiates PlayerAvatarController scene, applies replication snapshot, attaches to celestial frame if available
  • _register_avatar_collision_patch(avatar, planet) — Pre-registers fine-grained collision patch on planet surface before first physics tick
  • _update_avatar_surface_anchors() — Physics-process loop that updates all avatar anchors on their planets and configures high-resolution patches on first registration

docs.title: Disembark Flow (Server-Authoritative)
docs.text:
1. Client initiates: request_local_player_disembark() sends RPC to server via AvatarReplicationService
  2. Server validates: Checks ship can disembark, world instance matches, no existing authoritative avatar for peer
  3. Server reanchors: Calls ship._reanchor_landed_pose() to compute consistent spawn position against server terrain
  4. Server resolves spawn: Uses DisembarkSpawnResolver to find valid surface point near landed ship
  5. Server spawns avatar: Creates PlayerAvatarController in AuthoritativeAvatars root, attaches to celestial frame, registers collision patch
  6. Server broadcasts: Sends spawn payload to all combat-ready peers except the requester
  7. Client receives: handle_disembark_accepted() creates local avatar, applies snapshot, attaches input sender

docs.title: Reenter Flow
docs.text:
1. Client calls request_local_player_reenter_ship(ship)
  2. Server validates: avatar exists, ship is landed, avatar distance <= reenter_distance threshold
  3. If valid: despawns authoritative avatar, notifies other peers of despawn
  4. Client receives handle_reenter_ship_accepted() and applies possession transition

docs.title: Collision Patch System
docs.text: Avatar walking requires fine-grained terrain collision detail. The system pre-registers a 40m radius, 32-resolution heightmap patch on the planet surface at spawn time. This prevents avatars from falling onto the ship's coarse landing patch and being pushed to wrong heights. On each physics frame, _update_avatar_surface_anchors() updates all active avatar anchors and configures patches for newly spawned avatars.

docs.title: Frame Attachment
docs.text: Avatars are attached to CelestialFrame objects (orbiting bodies) so their positions stay synchronized with the moving body. If the client hasn't loaded the target frame yet, attachment is deferred via attach_to_surface_frame_deferred() — the avatar binds when the frame loads instead of spawning frameless and being left behind on an orbiting body.

docs.title: World Instance Isolation
docs.text: All avatar operations are scoped to a world_instance_id. The manager resolves this from WorldRuntimeLookup and validates that disembark requests match the current world. Peer combat-ready filtering ensures avatars are only sent to peers in the same world instance.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumes PlayerAvatarController for avatar instances
  • Uses DisembarkSpawnResolver for spawn point resolution
  • Integrates with AvatarReplicationService for RPC communication
  • Consumes PlayerPossession for disembark/reenter state transitions
  • Uses WorldRuntimeLookup for world instance resolution

docs.title: NetworkShipManager
docs.text: Server-side network ship management: snapshot broadcast, distance-based delivery policy, ShipSnapshotCodec integration.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: NetworkShipManager (scripts/network/network_ship_manager.gd) manages the server-side broadcasting of ship state snapshots to connected clients. It implements the authoritative server model with distance-based delivery policies and binary-packed snapshot encoding via ShipSnapshotCodec.

docs.title: Key Preloads
docs.text:
• ShipSnapshotCodecScript — binary packing/unpacking for snapshots
  • ShipSnapshotDeliveryPolicyScript / ShipSnapshotDeliveryStateScript — per-viewer delivery state
  • ShipWeaponSelectionDeliveryStateScript — weapon selection sync state
  • ShipSnapshotDebugFormatterScript — debug formatting utilities
  • ShipReplicationInterestPolicyScript / ShipReplicationVisibilityStateScript / ShipReplicationViewerStateScript — replication interest/visibility management

docs.title: Snapshot Broadcast Flow
docs.text: Physics tick (30Hz) → NetworkShipManager.collect_states() → ShipSnapshotCodec.pack_snapshot() → ShipReplicationService.broadcast() → Client receive

docs.title: Distance-Based Delivery Policy
docs.text: Distance Tick Divisor Bandwidth Impact Near (< 2500 LY) 1x (every frame) High Medium (2500–6000 LY) 2x (every other frame) Medium Far (> 6000 LY) 5x (every 5th frame) Low

docs.title: Binary Snapshot Format (ShipSnapshotCodec)
docs.text: Packed binary snapshot per ship: - Meta: 5 integers — peer_id, time_ms, flags_bitfield, weapon_type, sequence - Data: 23 floats — pos.xyz, quat.xyzw, vel.xyz, throttle, travel_spinup, beam_origin_pos.xyz, beam_target_pos.xyz, shield_current, hull_hp, highway_active, highway_lane_id, highway_progress - Total: ~108 bytes per ship per tick (via PACKED_SNAPSHOT_META_STRIDE + PACKED_SNAPSHOT_DATA_STRIDE)

docs.title: FlightHitchLog Diagnostics
docs.text:
When debug_flight_hitch = true or FlightHitchLog.is_trace_enabled(), the manager emits a per-second summary: - Server side: inputs_consumed, inputs_stale, registered ship count, physics/snapshot Hz, collision patch rebuild counts (concave/heightmap) - Client side: reconciliation corrections, snaps, offsets, suppressed events, first-time corrections, average/max offset distances

See docs/flight_latency_tasks.md Phase 1 for full diagnostics spec.

docs.title: Integration
docs.text:
• Called by: Server physics loop (_process() at 30Hz)
  • Sends to: ShipReplicationService for network transport
  • Receives from: Client RemoteShipController acknowledgments
  • Frame domain tracking: _terrain_contact_frame_state: FrameDomainState used by terrain contact solver integration

docs.title: Performance Notes
docs.text:
• Critical path for multiplayer performance
  • With N ships and M clients: O(N×M) broadcasts per tick
  • Distance-based policy reduces bandwidth significantly in typical scenarios
  • ShipSnapshotCodec binary format is ~60% smaller than raw dictionary serialization

Page created: 2026-05-25 | Source: scripts/network/network_ship_manager.gd

docs.title: NetworkSnapshotGenerator
docs.text: Server-side snapshot generator that compiles ship state data for network replication.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: NetworkSnapshotGenerator (scripts/systems/network_snapshot_generator.gd) runs every physics tick per ship, gathering flight model data, weapon states, and position/orientation into a packed binary format for broadcast via ShipReplicationService.

docs.title: Snapshot Compilation Flow
docs.text: Physics tick → NetworkSnapshotGenerator._process() → gather_ship_data() → pack_snapshot() → ShipReplicationService.broadcast()

docs.title: Data Gathered Per Ship
docs.text:
• Position, velocity, orientation (Quaternion)
  • Flight model state (boost/travel/highway mode)
  • Weapon firing states (runtime_is_firing() per weapon group)
  • Shield/fuel/cargo status
  • Player identity and target information

docs.title: MEDIUM — .call() in hot path
docs.text:
Runs every physics tick per ship. Uses .call() to check runtime_is_firing():

# Wrong: bypasses type resolution, has_method adds overhead
if weapon.has_method("runtime_is_firing"):
    firing = weapon.call("runtime_is_firing")

Fix: Call methods directly: weapons.runtime_is_firing(). Remove guards.

docs.title: Integration
docs.text:
• Called by: ShipReplicationService (server-side)
  • Data format: Packed binary — 5 meta ints + 23 data floats per ship
  • Broadcast rate: 30Hz (physics tick synchronized)
  • Delivery policy: Distance-based tick divisors (near/medium/far)

docs.title: Performance Notes
docs.text:
• Runs in server _process() every physics frame
  • With N ships and M weapon groups: O(N×M) .call() overhead per tick
  • Critical path for multiplayer latency — direct calls preferred

Page created: 2026-05-25 | Source: scripts/systems/network_snapshot_generator.gd

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Godot autoload singleton that simulates the full lifecycle of NPC actors (ships) across the galaxy when they are not in the player's active view. Handles registration, deregistration, promotion to active patrol, and demotion back to simulation — enabling persistent world state without loading every ship into the scene tree.

Class name: Not declared as class_name Base class: Node Location: scripts/autoload/npc_simulation_service.gd

docs.title: Key Responsibilities
docs.text:
1. Actor lifecycle — Registers actors when they enter simulation range, deregisters when they leave
  2. Promotion pipeline — Requests promotion of individual ships or entire wings into active patrol (via patrol-manager)
  3. Demotion pipeline — Handles demotion back to simulation when ships are no longer relevant
  4. Counter tracking — Maintains aggregate counters for active/dormant actors

Godot autoload singleton that manages NPC patrol wings across the game world. Handles wing lifecycle — creation, promotion/demotion of individual ships into/out of active patrols, and tracking member losses.

Class name: PatrolM (currently commented out as #class_name PatrolM) Base class: Node Location: scripts/autoload/patrol_manager.gd

docs.text:
1. Wing management — Tracks patrol wings via _wings: Dictionary mapping wing_id to PatrolWingData
  2. Actor assignment — Maps individual actors (ships) to their parent wings via _actor_to_wing: Dictionary
  3. Promotion/demotion — Handles the lifecycle of ships being promoted from simulation to active patrol and vice versa

docs.text:
• wing_promoted(wing_id, system_coords, world_instance_id) — A new patrol wing was formed

  • wing_member_lost(wing_id, member_id) — An NPC ship was removed from a patrol

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• class_name is commented out (#class_name PatrolM) — This means the class cannot be referenced by type annotation in other scripts. All callers must use .call() or dynamic access, which prevents compile-time safety and forces runtime string hashing.
  • See debugging for known GDScript pitfalls related to this pattern.

  • Consumed by faction-manager (FM) for faction-aware patrol assignment

docs.title: SurfaceInteractService
docs.text: File: scripts/player/player_surface_interaction.gd (193 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
SurfaceInteractService handles player-on-foot interaction with the game world while landed on planets and moons. It scans for nearby surface landmarks, cities, and points of interest within interact range, displays "Press F — {name}" prompts on the FPS HUD, and opens an info popup when the player interacts. The service manages target persistence during popup display to prevent accidental ship re-entry.

Class name: SurfaceInteractService Base class: Node Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/player/player_surface_interaction.gd (193 lines)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The SurfaceInteractService bridges the gap between the player avatar and interactive world objects on planetary surfaces. It provides proximity-based interaction detection, HUD prompt management, and popup lifecycle control for surface landmarks and POIs encountered during FPS exploration.

docs.title: Exported Properties
docs.text:
• interact_distance: float = 25.0 — Maximum distance (in meters) for detecting interactable objects
  • scan_interval: float = 0.3 — Time between proximity scans in seconds
  • target_groups: Array[StringName] — Groups to scan for interactables, defaulting to:
  • "nav/surface_landmark" — Named surface landmarks
  • "nav/surface_city" — Surface cities and settlements
  • "nav/poi" — General points of interest

docs.title: Internal State
docs.text:
• _local_avatar: PlayerAvatarController — Reference to the current local player avatar
  • _nearest_target: Node3D — The closest interactable object within range
  • _nearest_distance: float — Distance to the nearest target
  • _scan_accumulator: float — Timer for periodic scanning
  • _popup: CanvasLayer — Interaction popup instance (dynamically created)
  • _popup_open: bool — Whether an interaction popup is currently displayed

docs.title: Public API
docs.text:
• interact_distance (exported, float) — Configurable interact range
  • scan_interval (exported, float) — Configurable scan frequency
  • target_groups (exported, Array[StringName]) — Configurable groups to scan

docs.title: Lifecycle Methods
docs.text:
• _ready() — Connects to PlayerPossession.local_avatar_changed signal and initializes scanning. Logs configured target groups and interact distance.
  • _on_local_avatar_changed(avatar: Node3D) — Handles avatar transitions by disconnecting signals from the old avatar, connecting to the new one, and clearing any active target.

docs.title: Core Logic
docs.text:
• _process(delta: float) — Main update loop that:
  • Clears target if no valid avatar exists
  • Skips scanning while radial science menu is active (is_radial_menu_active())
  • Skips scanning while any other popup is open (via Game.is_any_popup_open())
  • Periodically scans nearby interactables at scan_interval rate

  • Handles the "fps_interact" input action to open interaction popups

  • _scan_nearby() — Iterates through all nodes in target_groups, computes distances from avatar, and updates _nearest_target if a closer valid target is found within interact_distance. Calls avatar.set_interact_target() to update HUD prompts.

  • _open_interaction_popup() — Creates the popup if needed (via POPUP_SCRIPT preload of res://scripts/ui/fps_interaction_popup.gd), calls show_entity(_nearest_target), and connects to the dismissed signal for cleanup.

docs.title: Popup Management
docs.text:
• _on_radial_menu_opened() — Closes any open interaction popup when the radial science menu opens, preventing UI conflicts.
  • _on_popup_dismissed() — Clears the interact target after popup dismissal, allowing normal ship re-entry to resume.
  • _clear_target() — Resets nearest target and distance, clears avatar's interact target reference, and resets scan accumulator.

docs.title: Avatar Lifecycle Management
docs.text: The service tracks the current local avatar through PlayerPossession.local_avatar_changed signals. On each avatar change, it properly disconnects all signal connections from the old avatar before connecting to the new one, preventing memory leaks and stale references.

docs.title: Popup Persistence Strategy
docs.text: When an interaction popup is open, the nearest target remains set on the avatar even though scanning is paused. This prevents the avatar's ship re-entry logic from firing accidentally while the player is reading information about a landmark or POI. The target is only cleared when the popup is dismissed.

docs.title: Input Action Guarding
docs.text: The service checks Game.is_any_popup_open() to avoid interfering with other active popups or instruments. It also skips scanning while the radial science menu is active, preventing UI conflicts between the interaction system and the instrument selection system.

docs.title: Dynamic Popup Creation
docs.text: The interaction popup (fps_interaction_popup.gd) is created dynamically on first use via POPUP_SCRIPT.new() and added to the tree root as a CanvasLayer named "SurfaceInteractionPopup". This lazy initialization avoids unnecessary scene loading when no surface interactions occur.

docs.title: Target Group System
docs.text: Interactable objects are organized into Godot node groups (nav/surface_landmark, nav/surface_city, nav/poi). Objects must have a "display_name" meta set for the HUD prompt to display meaningful text. The _find_group() helper identifies which group a target belongs to for debug logging.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Integrates with PlayerAvatarController for interact target management and radial menu awareness
  • Uses PlayerPossession autoload for avatar lifecycle tracking
  • Consumes Game singleton for popup state awareness
  • Creates instances of FpsInteractionPopup (res://scripts/ui/fps_interaction_popup.gd) for display
  • Scans nodes in groups defined by target_groups export

docs.title: PlayerPossessionService
docs.text: Manages transitions between ship control and avatar (on-foot) mode.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PlayerPossessionService (scripts/systems/player_possession_service.gd) handles the player's transition between controlling a ship (flight mode) and an avatar character (on-foot/inside stations).

docs.title: Possession States
docs.text:
SHIP_CONTROL → DISMOUNT_REQUEST → TRANSITION_ANIMATION → AVATAR_CONTROL
AVATAR_CONTROL → EMBARK_REQUEST → TRANSITION_ANIMATION → SHIP_CONTROL

docs.title: Key Methods
docs.text:
• request_dismount(ship) → initiates ship-to-avatar transition
  • request_embark(ship_data, spawn_position) → initiates avatar-to-ship transition
  • get_current_possession() → returns current mode (SHIP/AVATAR)
  • cancel_transition() → aborts pending transition

docs.title: Integration with PlayerAvatarController
docs.text: Works alongside: - PlayerAvatarController: Avatar movement, physics, and interaction - DisembarkSpawnResolver: Determines spawn position when leaving ship - ShipVisualProxy: Client-side visual representation during transitions

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• No explicit issues found in recent audits
  • Transition animations must be synchronized across network (see EventReplicationService)

docs.title: PredictionService
docs.text: Client-side prediction and interpolation service for smooth ship movement.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PredictionService (scripts/systems/prediction_service.gd) handles client-side prediction of ship state between server snapshots, reducing perceived latency in multiplayer flight.

docs.title: Prediction Flow
docs.text: Server snapshot (30Hz) → PredictionService.interpolate() → Client renders predicted position

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Interpolation buffer: Stores recent server states for smooth interpolation
  • Prediction offset: Applies client input immediately, corrects on server ack
  • Extrapolation budget: Max time before forced correction from server state

docs.title: Integration with SnapshotBuffer
docs.text: Works alongside SnapshotBuffer which handles quaternion slerp + linear lerp interpolation. PredictionService adds the prediction layer on top.

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• No explicit issues found in recent audits
  • Relies on SharedFlightModel for deterministic physics sync

Page created: 2026-05-25 | Source: scripts/systems/prediction_service.gd

docs.title: RadiationMeter
docs.text: File: scripts/player/radiation_meter.gd (47 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
RadiationMeter is a static utility class for computing surface radiation levels on planets and moons. It takes the planet's science profile, resource deposit information, and a scan seed to produce deterministic but varied radiation readings including counts per minute (CPM), radon detection, and radioactive deposit hints. Unlike physics-based instruments, this operates as a pure computation service called during scan completion.

Class name: RadiationMeter Base class: RefCounted (static utility) Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/player/radiation_meter.gd (47 lines)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The RadiationMeter provides radiation hazard assessment data for the player's surface environment. It determines whether conditions are safe, elevated, hazardous, or lethal, and detects secondary indicators like radon gas and radioactive mineral deposits that could boost radiation levels.

docs.title: Static Method
docs.text:
• measure(profile: PlanetScienceProfile, resource_deposits: Dictionary, scan_seed: int) -> Dictionary — Computes radiation data from planet profile and environmental factors. Returns a dictionary with these keys:
  • radiation_level (String) — One of "safe", "elevated", "hazardous", or "lethal"
  • radiation_cpm (float) — Counts per minute, snapped to 0.1 precision
  • radon_detected (bool) — True if radon is detected (only in elevated/hazardous zones with >50% chance)
  • radioactive_deposits_hinted (bool) — True if uranium/thorium deposits or random chance (>85%) suggests radioactive minerals

docs.title: CPM Ranges by Level
docs.text:
• "safe": 5.0 - 25.0 CPM
  • "elevated": 25.0 - 150.0 CPM
  • "hazardous": 150.0 - 800.0 CPM
  • "lethal": 800.0 - 5000.0 CPM

docs.title: Modifiers
docs.text:
• Random variation: +/-10% of base CPM value applied after initial range selection
  • Radioactive deposits bonus: If uranium, thororium, or random chance triggers deposits AND level is not "lethal", CPM is multiplied by 1.5x
  • Minimum floor: CPM is clamped to minimum 0.1

docs.title: Random Seed Determinism
docs.text: Uses a deterministic seed derived from hash("%s_radiation_%s" % [str(scan_seed), "rad_meter"]) so that repeated scans of the same location produce consistent results across sessions while varying between different scan seeds.

docs.title: Static Design Pattern
docs.text: RadiationMeter is a pure static utility with no instance state, similar to LidarMapper. It takes all input as parameters and returns results as a dictionary. This allows it to be called from any context during the scan pipeline without needing an instantiated object.

docs.title: Resource Deposit Integration
docs.text: The meter cross-references resource_deposits dictionary keys for "uranium" and "thorium" to provide environmental storytelling — radioactive deposits hint at both radiation hazards and valuable mineral resources.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Called by PlayerAvatarController after radiation scan initiation
  • Output consumed by FpsRadiationMeterWidget for display
  • Feeds into planet science dossier data aggregation
  • Uses PlanetScienceProfile.base_radiation_level as the primary input

docs.title: SeismicThumper
docs.text: File: scripts/player/seismic_thumper.gd (405 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
SeismicThumper is a physics-based FPS surface instrument that generates seismic pulses to map subsurface geological structure. It lands on the planet surface, enters a countdown phase, then listens for 15 seconds while generating simulated waveform data based on the planet's crust type and seismic activity level. The instrument produces detailed subsurface analysis including crust thickness estimates, tectonic activity classification, core type hints, and aftershock detection counts.

Class name: SeismicThumper Base class: RigidBody3D Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/player/seismic_thumper.gd (405 lines)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The SeismicThumper provides comprehensive subsurface geological analysis including crust type, estimated crust thickness in kilometers, tectonic activity level, core type hints, aftershock counts, and ring duration measurements. It is part of the FPS instrument suite accessible via the radial science menu.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text:
• seismic_measured(data: Dictionary, entity_id: String, display_name: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Emitted when the full 15-second listening phase completes with seismic data
  • seismic_progress(amplitude: float, time_remaining: float) — Emitted during countdown (amplitude=-1.0) and listening phases (amplitude=current waveform amplitude). Negative amplitude indicates countdown phase.
  • seismic_countdown(time_left: float) — Emitted during the 2-second pre-listen countdown
  • seismic_wave_sample(value: float) — Emitted for each individual waveform sample at 60 Hz
  • seismic_complete_waveform(waveform: PackedFloat64Array) — Emitted with the full collected waveform data

docs.title: Constants
docs.text:
• LISTEN_COUNTDOWN: float = 2.0 — Pre-listen countdown duration in seconds
  • LISTEN_DURATION: float = 15.0 — Active listening phase duration
  • RING_THRESHOLD: float = 0.06 — Amplitude threshold for ring detection
  • AFTERSHOCK_THRESHOLD: float = 0.25 — Amplitude threshold for aftershock/quakes detection
  • PROGRESS_INTERVAL: float = 0.25 — Time between progress signal emissions
  • SAMPLES_PER_SECOND: float = 60.0 — Waveform sampling rate

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: • configure(gravity_g: float, planet_node: Node3D, entity_id: String, display_name_val: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Sets up the instrument with gravity scaling and surface interest registration (patch_radius: 20.0, patch_resolution: 8). Bounce adjusted to 0.15 * bounce_scale.

docs.title: Seismic Parameter Setup
docs.text:
_setup_seismic_params() configures waveform parameters based on crust type from planet science profile: - "icy": ring_freq=4.0, ring_decay=0.6 (slow, long-lasting rings) - "metallic": ring_freq=12.0, ring_decay=3.0 (fast, short-lived rings) - "volcanic": ring_freq=6.0, ring_decay=1.2 (moderate) - Default/rocky: ring_freq=8.0, ring_decay=1.5

Aftershock chance per second is computed as 0.1 + activity * 0.8 where activity comes from profile.seismic_activity_level.

docs.title: Waveform Generation
docs.text: _generate_waveform_sample(delta) produces each waveform sample by combining: 1. Ring signal: Exponentially decaying sine wave (exp(-sim_time * ring_decay) * sin(sim_time * ring_freq * TAU)) scaled to 60% amplitude 2. Aftershock spikes: Random-amplitude, random-frequency transient events based on seismic activity level 3. Residual aftershock: Fading sine wave during aftershock decay period (0.2s) 4. Background noise: Uniform random in [-0.5, 0.5] scaled to 0.02

docs.title: Data Output Dictionary Keys
docs.text:
• seismic_activity_level (float) — From planet science profile
  • crust_type (String) — Crust classification ("rocky", "icy", "metallic", "volcanic")
  • crust_thickness_estimate_km (float) — Estimated crust thickness in kilometers
  • aftershocks_detected (int) — Number of aftershock/quakes detected during listening phase
  • tectonic_activity (String) — Tectonic activity classification from profile
  • core_type_hint (String) — Core type hint from planet science profile
  • ring_duration_s (float) — Duration of the main seismic ring in seconds

docs.title: Visual Feedback
docs.text:
• _trigger_impact_flash() — White flash on impact (emission 4.0x for 0.15s, then back to base blue emission)
  • _update_listen_visual(sample) — Antenna mesh wobble scales with sample amplitude; emission energy and color shift based on signal intensity

docs.title: Movement Control
docs.text: • kick(player_pos: Vector3, horizontal_dir: Vector3, strength: float = 20.0) — Kicks the instrument horizontally from player position. Resets _has_settled to allow re-triggering of the measurement cycle.

docs.title: Three-Phase Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Settle phase (0.3s) — Waits after landing before starting countdown
  2. Countdown phase (2.0s) — Pre-listen countdown with progress updates, amplitude=-1.0
  3. Listening phase (15.0s) — Active waveform collection at 60 Hz, generating ring + aftershock + noise signals

docs.title: Physics Integration
docs.text: Uses _integrate_forces() for frame-synced physics with body-relative velocity tracking and terrain clamping via sample_height_and_normal(). The same pattern as other FPS instruments for consistent behavior across moving bodies.

docs.title: Waveform Threshold Detection
docs.text:
• Ring detection: samples exceeding RING_THRESHOLD (0.06) update the ring end time
  • Aftershock detection: samples exceeding AFTERSHOCK_THRESHOLD (0.25) are recorded in _detected_quakes array

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by PlayerAvatarController for instrument deployment
  • Reads data from PlanetPreset.science_profile for crust type, seismic activity, tectonic level, and core hints
  • Integrates with FPS HUD via FpsHudOverlay for waveform visualization
  • Uses BodyFrameTransform for planet-relative positioning

docs.title: ShipSnapshotCodec
docs.text: File: scripts/network/ship_snapshot_codec.gd (143 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
ShipSnapshotCodec is a binary-packed ship state serialization system for network replication. It converts ship snapshot dictionaries into compact PackedInt64Array and PackedFloat32Array buffers, enabling efficient multiplayer state synchronization between client and server. The codec uses fixed-stride packing with validation checks for NaN/Inf values.

Class name: ShipSnapshotCodec Base class: RefCounted Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/network/ship_snapshot_codec.gd (143 lines)

docs.title: Constants
docs.text:
• PACKED_SNAPSHOT_META_STRIDE: int = 6 — Meta array element count per snapshot
  • PACKED_SNAPSHOT_DATA_STRIDE: int = 24 — Data array element count per snapshot

docs.title: pack_snapshot(meta, data, peer_id, snap) -> void
docs.text:
Packs a ship snapshot dictionary into meta and data arrays. The input snap dictionary contains raw ship state fields. Output is appended to the provided Packed*Array instances (caller manages buffer lifecycle).

Meta stride (6 PackedInt64): | Index | Field | Type | Description | |-------|-------|------|-------------| | 0 | peer_id | int | Replicating peer ID | | 1 | time_ms | int | Timestamp in milliseconds | | 2 | flags | int | Bitmask of ship state flags | | 3 | weapon_type | int | Current weapon type index | | 4 | sequence | int | Snapshot sequence number (-1 if none) | | 5 | frame_id | int | Celestial frame ID for position context |

Data stride (24 PackedFloat32): | Index | Field | Type | Description | |-------|-------|------|-------------| | 0-2 | position | Vector3 | Ship world position | | 3-6 | rotation_quat | Quaternion | Normalized rotation quaternion | | 7-9 | velocity | Vector3 | Ship world velocity | | 10 | throttle | float | Throttle value (0.0-1.0) | | 11 | spinup | float | Travel mode spinup progress | | 12-14 | beam_origin_pos | Vector3 | Beam weapon origin position | | 15-17 | beam_target_pos | Vector3 | Beam weapon target position (only if active) | | 18 | shield_current | float | Current shield HP | | 19 | hull_hp | float | Current hull HP | | 20 | highway_progress | float | Space highway lane progress | | 21-23 | local_position | Vector3 | Ship position in parent frame |

docs.title: Flags Bitmask
docs.text: Bit Value Field Description 0 1 boost Boost system active 1 2 travel_mode_active FTL/travel mode engaged 2 4 fire Weapon firing 3 8 beam_target_active Beam weapon targeting 4 16 destroyed Ship destroyed 5 32 flight_assist_enabled Flight assist on (default) 6 64 highway_active Space highway lane following

docs.title: unpack_snapshot(meta, meta_index, data, data_index) -> Dictionary
docs.text: Unpacks a single snapshot from packed arrays at the given indices. Returns a dictionary with all ship state fields. Handles: - Quaternion normalization (skips if length_squared < 0.000001) - Beam target position only decoded when beam_target_active flag is set - Bounds checking for sequence and frame_id meta offsets

docs.title: is_valid_packed_snapshot(meta, meta_index, data, data_index) -> bool
docs.text: Validates a packed snapshot before unpacking: - Checks meta stride bounds (meta_index + 6 <= meta.size()) - Checks data stride bounds (data_index + 24 <= data.size()) - Validates peer_id > 0 - Scans all 24 data floats for NaN or Inf values

docs.title: Buffer Management
docs.text: The codec operates on append-only Packed*Array instances. The caller (typically NetworkShipManager) manages buffer lifecycle, flushing packed snapshots to the network at regular intervals. Meta and data arrays are interleaved — each snapshot occupies exactly PACKED_SNAPSHOT_META_STRIDE + PACKED_SNAPSHOT_DATA_STRIDE = 30 total elements across both arrays.

docs.title: Quaternion Normalization
docs.text: Quaternions are normalized during packing to prevent drift from accumulated rotation errors. During unpacking, if the raw quaternion's length_squared is below a threshold (1e-6), it defaults to IDENTITY rather than dividing by near-zero.

docs.title: Beam Target Optimization
docs.text: Beam target position data is only included in the packed buffer when beam_target_active flag is set. This saves 3 float slots per snapshot for ships not using beam weapons, reducing bandwidth usage.

docs.text:

  • Integrated with PredictionService for client-side prediction

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: ShipWeapons is a ship-mounted weapon systems management component. It extends Node3D and handles weapon selection, firing modes (probe, plasma, rocket, limpet), ammo/energy management, targeting integration with TargetingManager, and damage calculations. Uses a runtime-based architecture where WeaponRuntime subclasses handle type-specific behavior for each weapon category.

docs.title: TargetingManager
docs.text: Combat targeting system: target groups, cycling, and lightweight NPC handling.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: TargetingManager (scripts/systems/targeting_manager.gd) manages combat targeting for both player and NPC ships. It handles target group selection, cycling through available targets, and lightweight NPC target management.

docs.title: Target Selection Flow
docs.text: Player input → TargetingManager.select_target() → CombatTargetingSolver.calculate_intercept() → ShipWeapons.fire_at()

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Target groups: Organizes enemies into tactical groups (squadrons, capital ships)
  • Cycling: Auto-cycle through targets when primary is destroyed
  • Lightweight handling: Optimized NPC target management for batch processing
  • Intercept calculation: Delegates to CombatTargetingSolver for quadratic intercept point solving

docs.title: Integration with CombatSystem
docs.text: Works alongside: - ShipWeapons: Actual weapon firing logic (2,777 lines) - CombatTargetingSolver: Intercept point quadratic solver - SharedFlightModel: Deterministic physics for target prediction

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• No explicit issues found in recent audits
  • Uses .call() pattern in some areas (see audit findings for .call() hot paths)

docs.title: ThermalPenetrator
docs.text: File: scripts/player/thermal_penetrator.gd (270 lines)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
ThermalPenetrator is a physics-based FPS surface instrument that measures thermal properties of planetary terrain and subsurface. It launches upward from the player's position, descends to land on the surface, then drives a spike into the ground over an 8-second penetration period while collecting temperature data. The instrument provides surface temperature, geothermal gradient, geothermal activity detection, and permafrost presence information.

Class name: ThermalPenetrator Base class: RigidBody3D Location: /home/j/3d-space/scripts/player/thermal_penetrator.gd (270 lines)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The ThermalPenetrator provides thermal analysis data for surface and subsurface temperature conditions. It is part of the FPS instrument suite accessible via the radial science menu, helping players assess environmental hazards and geothermal resource potential.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text:
• temperature_measured(data: Dictionary, entity_id: String, display_name: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Emitted when penetration completes with thermal data
  • penetration_progress(ratio: float) — Emitted during the 8-second penetration phase at discrete steps (0.125 increments)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: • configure(gravity_g: float, planet_node: Node3D, entity_id: String, display_name_val: String, system_coords: Vector3i) — Sets up the instrument with gravity scaling and surface interest registration (patch_radius: 20.0, patch_resolution: 8). Bounce adjusted to 0.2 * bounce_scale.

docs.title: Penetration Phase
docs.text: After landing (_has_settled becomes true), the instrument enters an 8-second penetration phase: - _settle_timer increments each physics frame - Progress ratio = min(settle_timer / 8.0, 1.0) - At completion (ratio=1.0), spike is fully extended and data is emitted

docs.title: Data Output Dictionary Keys
docs.text:
• surface_temperature_c (float) — Surface temperature in Celsius from planet science profile
  • geothermal_gradient (float) — Temperature increase rate with depth from profile
  • has_geothermal_activity (bool) — Whether the planet has active geothermal features
  • has_permafrost (bool) — Whether permafrost is present

docs.title: Visual Feedback
docs.text:
• _update_penetration_visuals(progress) — Spike mesh Y-scale grows from ~0.001 to 0.8 over penetration duration; emission energy increases from base (0.8x) to hot (2.0x); emission color shifts toward warmer tones
  • _trigger_impact_flash() — White flash on impact (emission 4.0x for 0.15s, then back to base orange emission Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.05))

docs.title: Movement Control
docs.text: • kick(player_pos: Vector3, horizontal_dir: Vector3, strength: float = 20.0) — Kicks the instrument horizontally from player position. Resets _has_settled to allow re-triggering of the measurement cycle.

docs.title: Physics Integration
docs.text: Uses _integrate_forces() for frame-synced physics with body-relative velocity tracking and terrain clamping via sample_height_and_normal(). Same pattern as other FPS instruments.

docs.title: Penetration Visual Design
docs.text: The spike ($SpikePivot/Spike) visually extends during penetration, providing clear feedback to the player about measurement progress. The emission color shifts from orange base toward warmer tones as penetration deepens, simulating increasing heat exposure.

docs.title: Progress Emission Strategy
docs.text: Progress signals are emitted at discrete 1/8 steps (every ~1 second) rather than continuously, reducing signal overhead while maintaining smooth visual updates through interpolation in the HUD widget.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Consumed by PlayerAvatarController for instrument deployment
  • Reads data from PlanetPreset.science_profile for thermal parameters
  • Integrates with FPS HUD via FpsHudOverlay for progress visualization and temperature display
  • Uses BodyFrameTransform for planet-relative positioning
